[$HTTP_RAW_POST_DATA](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$HTTP_RAW_POST_DATA.ini.html): ``$HTTP_RAW_POST_DATA`` used to contain the raw POST data of an HTTP request.
[$_FILES](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_FILES.ini.html): The ``$_FILES`` variable is a special superglobal variable that is used to retrieve information about files uploaded to the server via HTML forms with the ``enctype="multipart/form-data"`` attribute.
[$_COOKIE](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_cookie.ini.html): The ``$_COOKIE`` variable is a special superglobal variable that stores the name and values of the cookies that were transmitted to the webserver.
[$_ENV](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_env.ini.html): The ``$_ENV`` variable is a special superglobal variable that is used to retrieve the environment values.
[$_GET](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_get.ini.html): The ``$_GET`` variable is a special superglobal variable that is used to retrieve data from the query string or URL parameters.
[$_POST](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_post.ini.html): ``$_POST`` is an associative array of variables, transmitted to the current script via the HTTP POST method, with the MIME types ``application/x-www-form-urlencoded`` or ``multipart/form-data``.
[$_REQUEST](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_request.ini.html): An associative array of variables, transmitted to the current script via the HTTP GET and HTTP methods.
[$_SERVER](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$_server.ini.html): The ``$_SERVER`` variable is a special superglobal variable that holds information about the headers, scripts and path locations.
[$argc](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$argc.ini.html): ``$argc`` is a predefined variable that holds the number of arguments passed to the script when it is executed from the command line.
[$argv](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$argv.ini.html): ``$argv`` is a PHP variable that contains an array of the arguments passed to the PHP script, in the command line.
[$GLOBALS](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$globals.ini.html): This super-global is a PHP predefined variable, which holds a reference to every global variable in the application.
[$http_response_header](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$http_response_header.ini.html): ``$http_response_header`` contains the list of HTTP headers, as returned after a remote URI call, with ``fopen()`` or ``file_get_contents()``.
[$php_errormsg](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$php_errormsg.ini.html): ``$php_errormsg`` contains the last error message, emitted by PHP.
[$this](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/$this.ini.html): ``$this`` is a variable that represents the current object instance.
[__autoload](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__autoload.ini.html): ``__autoload()`` was the name of the userland function, used to load classes when they were not found yet in the current execution environment.
[__call() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__call.ini.html): ``__call()`` is a magic method:
[__callStatic() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__callstatic.ini.html): ``__callStatic()`` is a magic method:
[__CLASS__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__class__.ini.html): ``__CLASS__`` holds the current class name:
[__clone() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__clone.ini.html): To clone an object is the creation of a new distinct object, from an existing one.
[__debugInfo() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__debuginfo.ini.html): ``__debugInfo()`` is a magic method:
[__DIR__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__dir__.ini.html): ``__DIR__`` holds the directory of the current file.
[__FILE__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__file__.ini.html): ``__FILE__`` holds the current file name.
[__FUNCTION__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__function__.ini.html): ``__FUNCTION__`` holds the current function or method name.
[__get() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__get.ini.html): ``__get()`` is a magic method:
[__invoke() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__invoke.ini.html): The ``__invoke()`` magic method is called when the code calls an object as a function name.
[__isset() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__isset.ini.html): ``__isset()`` is a magic method:
[__LINE__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__line__.ini.html): ``__LINE__`` holds the current line number in the current file.
[__METHOD__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__method__.ini.html): ``__METHOD__`` holds the current method or function name.
[__serialize() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__serialize.ini.html): The ``__serialize()`` magic method is called when a script tries to construct an associative array of key/value pairs that represent the serialized form of the object.
[__set() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__set.ini.html): ``__set()`` is a magic method:
[__set_state() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__set_state.ini.html): ``__set_state()`` is a magic method:
[__sleep() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__sleep.ini.html): ``__sleep()`` is a magic method:
[__toString() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__tostring.ini.html): ``__tostring()`` is a magic method:
[__TRAIT__](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__trait__.ini.html): ``__TRAIT__`` holds the current trait name:
[__unserialize() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__unserialize.ini.html): The ``__unserialize()`` magic method is called when a script tries to construct an object from an associative array of key/value pairs.
[__unset() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__unset.ini.html): ``__unset()`` is a magic method:
[__wakeup() Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/__wakeup.ini.html): ``__wakeup()`` is a magic method:
[abs()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abs.ini.html): ``abs()`` calculates the absolute value of a number.
[Absolute Name](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/absolute-name.ini.html): An absolute name is a name that is independent from the current namespace.
[Absolute Value](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/absolute-value.ini.html): An ``absolute`` value is the positive value of a real number.
[Absolute](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/absolute.ini.html): An ``absolute`` value is independent from the context in which it operates.
[Abstract Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstract-class.ini.html): An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated directly.
[Abstract Factory](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstract-factory.ini.html): An Abstract Factory is a design pattern that provides an interface for creating families of related or dependent objects without specifying their concrete classes.
[Abstract Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstract-method.ini.html): An abstract method is a method that has no code.
[Abstract Keyword](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstract.ini.html): PHP has ``abstract`` classes, properties and methods.
[Abstraction Layer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstraction-layer.ini.html): An abstraction layer is a piece of software that offer the same API to access multiple similar components.
[Abstraction](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/abstraction.ini.html): An abstraction, or also an abstraction layer, is a way to hide the underlying complexity of some logic behind a simpler interface so that other parts of the program don't need to worry about the details.
[Accessibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/accessibility.ini.html): Accessibility is the process to make the code accessible to everyone.
[Accountability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/accountability.ini.html): Accountability isn't a built-in language feature:
[ACID](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/acid.ini.html): ACID is an acronym for the four properties that guarantee reliable database transactions.
[Access Control List (ACL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/acl.ini.html): An Access Control List, or ACL, is a set of rules that defines which users or system processes are granted access to specific resources, and what operations they are allowed to perform.
[Active Record](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/active-record.ini.html): Active Record is a design pattern commonly used in development.
[Actor Model](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/actor-model.ini.html): The Actor Model, introduced by Carl Hewitt in 1973, is a mathematical model of concurrent computation.
[Adapters](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/adapter.ini.html): Adapters are the link between the application and the rest of the universe.
[Addition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/addition.ini.html): PHP additions apply to two different types:
[Architectural Decision Record (ADR)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/adr.ini.html): An Architectural Decision Record, or ADR, is a concise document that captures an important architectural decision made during the development of a software project.
[Agile](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/agile.ini.html): Agile refers to a set of software development methodologies that emphasize iterative delivery, collaboration, and adaptability.
[Artificial Intelligence (AI)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ai.ini.html): A neural network is a software made of many simple processing units, called neurons, connected and organized in layers.
[AJAX](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ajax.ini.html): AJAX, the Asynchronous JavaScript and XML feature, is a set of web development techniques that allow a web page to communicate with a server asynchronously, without requiring a full page reload.
[Algebraic Data Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/algebraic-data-type.ini.html): An algebraic data type is a composite type built by combining simpler types through two fundamental operations:
[Algorithm](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/algorithm.ini.html): An algorithm is a step-by-step procedure or set of rules designed to perform a specific task or solve a particular problem.
[Algorithmic Complexity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/algorithmic-complexity.ini.html): Algorithmic complexity, also called computational complexity, refers to the study of the resources required by an algorithm to solve a problem as a function of the input size.
[Alias Types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/alias-types.ini.html): An alias type is a short name for several types at once.
[Alias](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/alias.ini.html): An alias is a distinct name for an existing named structure.
[Allow Dynamic Properties](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/allowdynamicproperties.ini.html): This is a native PHP attribute, which tells the engine that a class can dynamically create properties without declaring them.
[Alternative Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/alternative-syntax.ini.html): PHP offers an alternative syntax for some of its control structures; namely, ``if``, ``while``, ``for``, ``foreach``, and ``switch``.
[AMP (Asynchronous Markup Protocol)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/amp.ini.html): AMP refers to the AMP PHP library, formerly Amp Project, an event-driven, non-blocking I/O framework for building asynchronous applications.
[Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/analysis.ini.html): Analysis in software development refers to the systematic examination of source code, runtime behaviour, or architecture to detect issues, measure quality, or derive understanding.
[Anchor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anchor.ini.html): An anchor is an HTML element ``<a>`` used to create hyperlinks, allowing navigation to other pages, resources, or sections within the same page.
[And Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/and.ini.html): ``and`` is the logical operator:
[Anemic Domain Model](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anemic-domain-model.ini.html): The anemic domain model is an anti-pattern identified by Martin Fowler where domain objects, contain only data, getters, setters, and no business logic.
[Annotations](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/annotation.ini.html): PHP annotations are a system to provide meta data about code, in a way that is programmatically processable.
[Anonymize](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymize.ini.html): To anonymize is the process to replace a precise identifier with an unrelated piece of data.
[Anonymous Catch](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymous-catch.ini.html): Traditionally, the catch statement requires specifying both an Exception class and an identifier variable to hold the caught exception.
[Anonymous Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymous-class.ini.html): A class may be instantiated, without defining a class name.
[Anonymous Constant](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymous-constant.ini.html): A constant may be anonymous.
[Anonymous Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymous-function.ini.html): A function may be instantiated, without defining a function name.
[Anonymous](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anonymous.ini.html): An anonymous structure is a structure that usually has a name, but may also go without it.
[Anti-Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/anti-pattern.ini.html): An anti-pattern is a common but counterproductive solution to a recurring problem.
[ANTLR4](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/antlr4.ini.html): ANTLR, ANother Tool for Language Recognition, currently at its 4th major version, is a parser generator:
[Ahead Of Time](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/aot.ini.html): Ahead-of-Time, AOT, compilation is the process of compiling source code to machine code before execution, as opposed to at runtime.
[API Gateway](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/api-gateway.ini.html): An API gateway is a server that acts as the single entry point for all client requests to a backend system.
[Application Programming Interface (API)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/api.ini.html): An API is a set of functions and procedures allowing the creation of applications that access the features or data of another service.
[Application Performance Management (APM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/apm.ini.html): APM stands for Application Performance Management.
[Application Server](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/app-server.ini.html): An application server is a software environment that handles the execution of PHP scripts, serving dynamic content and managing interactions between the web server, the backend systems, like databases, and the client, browser or API consumer.
[Appeasement Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/appeasement-pattern.ini.html): The appeasement pattern is a code smell where validation, error checking, or security controls are weakened or bypassed to satisfy code quality tools, a demanding caller or to silence errors, instead of fixing the underlying issue.
[Array Append](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/append.ini.html): Append is an array operation, which adds an element at the end of the array.
[Application](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/application.ini.html): An application is a software program designed to perform specific tasks for end users.
[Arbitrary Number Of Argument](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arbitrary-argument.ini.html): PHP allows any number of arguments to be passed to a functioncall.
[Arcane](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arcane.ini.html): Arcane refers to weird and obscure features, that are unknown, unused or forgotten:
[Architecture](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/architecture.ini.html): Architecture refers to the global design of code, or it servers.
[Archive](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/archive.ini.html): Archives are a single document that stores a collection of files and directories.
[Argon2](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/argon2.ini.html): Argon2 is a modern, secure, and highly efficient password hashing algorithm.
[Argument](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/argument.ini.html): Arguments are values passed to a function or method, at execution time.
[ArgumentCountError](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/argumentcounterror.ini.html): ``ArgumentCountError`` is thrown when too few arguments are passed to a user-defined or PHP native function or method.
[ArithmeticError Error](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arithmeticerror.ini.html): ``ArithmeticError`` is thrown when an error occurs while performing mathematical operations.
[Arity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arity.ini.html): Arity is the number of arguments, or operands, that a function, method, or operator accepts:
[Arm](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arm.ini.html): An arm is a branch in a ``match()`` expression.
[Callable Arrays](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-callable.ini.html): An callable array is an array with index ``0`` and ``1``.
[Array Callback](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-callback.ini.html): An array callback, is an array of two elements, which may be used as a callback function.
[Array With Curly Braces](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-curly-braces.ini.html): Curly braces used to be a feature, used to access specific index in an array or in a string.
[Array Dot Notation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-dot-notation.ini.html): OWASP stands for Open Web Application Security Project.
[Array Element](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-element.ini.html): An array element is a piece of data, stored at an index in the array.
[Array Shape](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-shape.ini.html): An array shape is a type annotation that describes an array by its exact set of keys and the type of the value stored under each one, instead of describing it only by the type of its elements in general.
[Array Spread](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-spread.ini.html): Array spread is the ellipsis operator, applied to an array.
[Array Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array-syntax.ini.html): The array syntax is the usage of square brackets after a data container, such as variables or properties, or a literal, to access an element.
[Array, []](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array.ini.html): ``array`` is the array structure.
[array_column](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_column.ini.html): ``array_column()`` extracts the index or property of the second argument from a list of arrays or objects passed as first argument.
[array_combine()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_combine.ini.html): ``array_combine()`` creates an array from an array of keys and an array of values.
[array_fill()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_fill.ini.html): ``array_fill()`` is a PHP native function which creates an array with ``$count`` times a specified ``$value``, starting at integer offset ``$start_index``.
[array_filter()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_filter.ini.html): ``array_filter()`` is a native PHP function that filters elements of an array using a callback.
[array_find()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_find.ini.html): ``array_find()`` is a PHP native function, introduced in version 8.
[array_find_key()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_find_key.ini.html): ``array_find_key()`` is a PHP native function, introduced in version 8.
[array_is_list()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_is_list.ini.html): ``array_is_list()`` is a native PHP function that checks if the given array is a list.
[array_key_exists()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_key_exists.ini.html): ``array_key_exists()`` reports if a key has been assigned any value in an array.
[array_key_first()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_key_first.ini.html): ``array_key_first()`` is a native PHP function that returns the first key of an array, or null if the array is empty.
[array_keys()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_keys.ini.html): ``array_keys()`` returns all keys, both integer and string, as a new numerically indexed array.
[array_map()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_map.ini.html): ``array_map()`` is a PHP native function that applies a closure to every element of an array.
[array_merge()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_merge.ini.html): ``array_merge()`` combines all the arrays passed as argument into one argument.
[array_pad()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_pad.ini.html): ``array_pad()`` is a PHP native function which completes an array up to ``$length`` elements with a specified ``$value``.
[array_pop()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_pop.ini.html): ``array_pop()`` removes the last inserted element in an array, and returns it.
[array_push()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_push.ini.html): ``array_push()`` adds the second argument, and all the next, to the end of the array, passed as first argument.
[array_search](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_search.ini.html): ``array_search()`` is a native PHP function that searches an array for a value and returns the corresponding key if found, or ``false`` if not found.
[array_shift()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_shift.ini.html): ``array_shift()`` is a PHP native function that removes and returns the first element of an array.
[array_slice()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_slice.ini.html): ``array_slice()`` is a PHP native function that extracts a portion of an array.
[array_splice()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_splice.ini.html): ``array_splice()`` is a PHP native function that removes a portion of an array.
[array_unique()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_unique.ini.html): The ``array_unique()`` PHP native function removes duplicate values from an array.
[array_unshift()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_unshift.ini.html): ``array_unshift()`` prepends one or more elements to the beginning of an array.
[array_values()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_values.ini.html): The ``array_values()`` PHP native function returns the values in an array, with automatically generated indexes.
[array_walk()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/array_walk.ini.html): ``array_walk()`` is a PHP native function that applies a closure to every element of an array.
[ArrayAccess](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arrayaccess.ini.html): ``ArrayAccess`` is an interface that allows objects to be accessed like arrays using square brackets, similar to accessing elements in a regular array.
[ArrayObject](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arrayobject.ini.html): ``ArrayObject`` is a native class that provides the functionality of an array with the added features of an object.
[Arrow Functions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/arrow-function.ini.html): Arrow functions are a type of closure with a specific syntax.
[Artifact](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/artifact.ini.html): An artifact refers to any intermediate or final output that is produced during the development process.
[As](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/as.ini.html): The as operator has several usage:
[American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ascii.ini.html): ASCII is a character encoding standard used to represent text in computers and electronic devices.
[Aspect-Oriented](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/aspect-oriented.ini.html): Aspect-oriented design structures code so that cross-cutting concerns:
[Aspect Oriented Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/aspect-programming.ini.html): Aspect programming is a programming paradigm that separate business logic with cross-cutting concerns:
[Assertions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/assertion.ini.html): Assertions are checks that take effect in development and testing environments, but are optimised away to have zero cost in production.
[Asset](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/asset.ini.html): Web assets, or also assets, is an encompassing term, that designates all essential elements necessary for the successful execution of a web-based project.
[Assignations](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/assignation.ini.html): Assignation refers to the process of assigning a value to a variable.
[Assignment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/assignment.ini.html): Assignment is the fundamental operation of binding a value to a variable.
[Associative Array](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/associative-array.ini.html): An associative array is an array whose keys are strings; there may also be integer index, mixed with the string ones.
[Assumption](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/assumption.ini.html): An assumption is a piece of code that makes use of knowledge that is not visible in the source.
[Abstract Syntactic Tree (AST)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ast.ini.html): An abstract syntax tree, also called an AST, is a tree representation of the abstract syntactic structure of PHP source code.
[Asymmetric Cryptography](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/asymmetric-cryptography.ini.html): Asymmetric cryptography, also called public-key cryptography, is a method of encrypting and authenticating data using two mathematically linked keys:
[Asymmetric Property](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/asymmetric-property.ini.html): An asymmetric property is a property that specify the asymmetric visibility.
[Asymmetric Visibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/asymmetric-visibility.ini.html): Asymmetric visibility is a feature of PHP class properties.
[Async](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/async.ini.html): The ``async`` keyword is used in programming, and especially in languages like JavaScript, Python, and C#, to define a function that runs asynchronously.
[Asynchronous](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/asynchronous.ini.html): Asynchronous PHP, often abbreviated as async PHP, refers to the capability of executing PHP code asynchronously, meaning that tasks can run concurrently without waiting for each other to complete.
[Atomic Operation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/atomic-operation.ini.html): An atomic operation is an operation that is indivisible:
[Attack Surface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/attack-surface.ini.html): The attack surface of an application is the sum of all the different points where an attacker could try to enter data, extract data, or trigger unintended behavior.
[Attack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/attack.ini.html): An attack on a server is any deliberate attempt to exploit, disrupt, or gain unauthorized access to a server, its data, or its services.
[Attribute Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/attribute-class.ini.html): This is a native PHP attribute, which tells the engine that a class is a PHP attribute.
[Attribute](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/attribute.ini.html): Attributes are machine readable options that may be added PHP code.
[Audit Trail](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/audit-trail.ini.html): An audit trail, or audit log, is a chronological record of events, actions, or changes that provides documentary evidence of who did what, when, and why in a system, process, or organization.
[Audit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/audit.ini.html): An audit is a systematic, independent, and documented process for obtaining evidence and evaluating it objectively to determine whether predefined criteria, policies, or standards are being met.
[Authentication](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/authentication.ini.html): Authentication is the proof that a user is indeed who he claims to be.
[Authorize](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/authorize.ini.html): To authorize means to give permission or approval for someone to do something, or to officially empower them to take a certain action.
[Preappend File Directive](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/auto_prepend_file.ini.html): The ``auto_prepend_file`` PHP directive adds an ``require`` call before the main file.
[Autocompletion](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/autocompletion.ini.html): Autocompletion is an IDE or editor feature that suggests or completes code while the developer is typing.
[Class Autoloading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/autoload.ini.html): Autoloading provides a hook to allow the loading of a class, the first time PHP requires it.
[Automagic](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/automagic.ini.html): Automagic is a porte-manteau, merging automatic and magic.
[Automatic Index](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/automatic-index.ini.html): Automatic index is the index that PHP assign to a value, when it is added to an array, without specifying the index.
[Autovivification](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/autovivification.ini.html): Autovivification is the automatic creation of an array when a value is dereferenced.
[Autowiring](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/autowiring.ini.html): Autowiring is the ability of the framework to inject dependencies, based on the constructor signature.

[Bitfield](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bitfield.ini.html): A bitfield is a data structure that packs multiple related boolean flags or small integers into a single integer or sequence of bits, where each bit or group of bits represents a distinct value or state.
[Bitmask](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bitmask.ini.html): A bitmask is a single integer, or sequence of bits, used to set, clear, or check the state of specific bits in another integer, usually via bitwise operations.
[Bitshift Operators](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bitshift-operator.ini.html): Bitshift operators ``<<`` and ``>>`` move bits within an integer.

[Blocking Wait](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/blocking-wait.ini.html): A blocking wait, or blocking call, is an operation that suspends the execution of the current process or thread until the operation completes.
[Bloom Filter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bloom-filter.ini.html): A Bloom filter is a space-efficient probabilistic data structure used to test whether an element is a member of a set.
[Body](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/body.ini.html): The body, of a function or a command, is the related block that is commanded by the command.
[Boilerplate](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/boilerplate.ini.html): Boilerplate refers to sections of code, documentation, or configuration that are repeated in multiple places with little or no variation.

[Bootstrap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bootstrap.ini.html): The bootstrap concept generally means starting something using minimal initial resources and building it up by using the results of earlier steps.

[Bottleneck](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/bottleneck.ini.html): A bottleneck is a place in a piece of software which cannot keep up with the rest of the application.

[Cache Stampede](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cache-stampede.ini.html): A cache stampede, also known as thundering herd or dog-piling, is a failure mode that occurs when many concurrent requests simultaneously discover that a cached value has expired or is missing.

[Code Architecture](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-architecture.ini.html): Code architecture refers to the high-level design and organization of a software system.
[Code Coverage](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-coverage.ini.html): Code coverage lists all the source code lines that were used while executing a test.

[Code Freeze](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-freeze.ini.html): A code freeze is a common practice in software development where all changes to the source code are temporarily halted or restricted for a specific period, typically leading up to a major release or milestone.
[Code Generation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-generation.ini.html): Code generation is the automatic production of source code from a higher-level specification, model, schema, or set of rules.
[Code Generator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-generator.ini.html): A code generator is a tool or library that automates the creation of repetitive, boilerplate, or complex code based on predefined templates, configurations, best practices or rules.
[Code Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-injection.ini.html): A CODE injection is a vulnerability, where external data is used as PHP code.
[Code Reuse](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-reuse.ini.html): Code reuse, as in any programming language, refers to the practice of writing code that can be used in multiple parts of an application or across different projects.
[Code Review](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-review.ini.html): Code review is a systematic process where developers examine each other's code to identify mistakes, improve quality, check local values usage and ensure adherence to best practices.
[Code Smell](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/code-smell.ini.html): A code smell refers to any characteristic or symptom in source code that indicates a potential problem or violation of good programming practices.
[Codepoint](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/codepoint.ini.html): A code point is a numerical value that represents a specific character in an encoding system, such as Unicode.
[Coding Conventions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/coding-convention.ini.html): Coding conventions are a set of guidelines for PHP that recommend source code writing, programming style and practices.
[Type Coercion](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/coercion.ini.html): Type coercion is the automatic conversion of a value to match a declared type, when it does not already have that type.
[Cognitive Complexity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cognitive-complexity.ini.html): Cognitive complexity is a way of measuring how difficult a piece of code is for a human to understand.
[Cohesion](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cohesion.ini.html): Cohesion measures how closely related and focused the responsibilities of a single module, class, or function are.
[Cold Start](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cold-start.ini.html): A cold start is the delay incurred when a serverless function or containerised service must be initialised from scratch before handling a request.
[Collection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/collection.ini.html): A collection is an array in which only elements of the same type can be stored.
[Collision](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/collision.ini.html): A collision happens when two entities of the same type have two distinct definitions.
[Colon](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/colon.ini.html): Colons are used in a variety of situations:
[Column Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/column-database.ini.html): A column database (or column-oriented / columnar database) stores data by column rather than by row.
[Comma](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/comma.ini.html): Commas are syntax elements, to separate elements when provided as a list.
[Command Bus](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/command-bus.ini.html): A command bus is a design pattern used in software development, particularly in the context of Domain-Driven Design (DDD) and CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation).
[Command](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/command.ini.html): The Command design pattern encapsulates a request as an object, allowing it to be stored, queued, logged, or undone.
[Comments](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/comment.ini.html): PHP comments are used to add explanatory notes or annotations within the code.
[Commit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/commit.ini.html): A commit is a validation of changes.
[Community](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/community.ini.html): The PHP community is the global network of developers, contributors, and enthusiasts who use, improve, and promote PHP.
[Compact Array](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compact-array.ini.html): An array is compact, or packed, if it only uses automated indices, starting with 0.
[compact()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compact.ini.html): ``compact()`` is a native PHP function, which creates an array containing variables and their values, from a list of variable names.
[Comparison](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/comparison.ini.html): Comparison operators check how two values relate to each other.
[Compatibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compatibility.ini.html): Compatibility refers to the ability of two or more things to work together without conflicts or issues.
[Compensation Logic](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compensation-logic.ini.html): Compensation logic refers to the set of actions taken to undo or neutralise the effects of a previously completed step when a subsequent step in a distributed process fails.
[Compile-time Evaluation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compile-time-evaluation.ini.html): Compile-time evaluation is the ability of a compiler to execute expressions or functions during compilation, embedding the computed result directly in the compiled output rather than deferring it to runtime.
[Compile Time](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compile-time.ini.html): Compile time is a phase of PHP processing, where the source code is transformed into execution commands.
[Compile](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compile.ini.html): To compile, or compilation, is the process to turn the source code into machine code for direct execution.
[Compiler](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compiler.ini.html): A compiler is a program that translates source code into another form, typically machine code or bytecode.
[Complexity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/complexity.ini.html): Complexity refers to how difficult the code is to understand, maintain, test, and modify.
[Compliance](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compliance.ini.html): Compliance generally refers to adhering to a set of predefined rules, standards, or regulations.
[Component](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/component.ini.html): A component is a part or element that combines with others to form a larger system, structure, or product.
[Composer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/composer.ini.html): Composer is the most popular package manager for PHP.
[Composite Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/composite-type.ini.html): Composite types combine multiple types into a single type expression.
[Composition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/composition.ini.html): Composition is the creation of new classes, by including other classes, and delivering a specific interface over them.
[Compression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/compression.ini.html): Compression is the representation of data in a format that uses less space that the original raw data.
[Computed Property](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/computed-property.ini.html): A computed property is a property whose value is derived from an expression at the point of access, with no explicit getter method in user code.
[Concatenation .](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/concatenation.ini.html): Concatenation is the PHP feature that appends two strings together, to make a third, longer one.
[Concatenative](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/concatenative.ini.html): A concatenative structure is a structure that is valid after placing values one after the other.
[Concrete Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/concrete-method.ini.html): A concrete method is a method that has code.
[Concrete Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/concrete.ini.html): PHP has abstract classes and methods:
[Concurrency](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/concurrency.ini.html): Concurrency is a concept in computing where multiple tasks are in progress at the same time.
[Condition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/condition.ini.html): Conditions are an expression that are either true or false.
[Conditional Structures](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/conditional-structure.ini.html): Conditional structures are instructions which run different code, based on a condition.
[Conditional](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/conditional.ini.html): Conditional means that some condition must be met for something to happen or to be.
[Conditioned Structures](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/conditioned.ini.html): Conditioned structures are definitions that are placed inside a condition structure.
[Configuration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/configuration.ini.html): Configuration is a set of data that conditions the execution of the program.
[Connection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/connection.ini.html): Connection refers to the communication link established between a running instance of PHP and a remote server.
[Connection Persistence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/connexion-persistence.ini.html): Connection persistence refers to the practice of reusing the same database connection across multiple requests, rather than opening and closing a connection for each individual request.
[Consistency](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/consistency.ini.html): Consistency means writing code that follows predictable patterns, standards, and conventions across a project or team.
[Const](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/const.ini.html): ``const`` is a PHP keyword, that creates constant definitions.
[Constant Case](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-case.ini.html): Constant case, also written ``CONSTANT_CASE`` is a way of writing constant names, without spaces, where:
[Constant Combination](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-combinaison.ini.html): PHP combines predefined constants using bitwise operators.
[Constant Expression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-expression.ini.html): A constant expression is an expression built with others constants and a limited subset of PHP operators.
[constant()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-function.ini.html): The ``constant()`` function is a native function, which provide the value of a declared global constant, a class constant or an enumeration case.
[Constants In Trait](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-in-trait.ini.html): Constants were added to trait in version 8.
[Constant Scalar Expression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant-scalar-expression.ini.html): A constant scalar expression is an expression that can be evaluated at compile time and consists only of scalar values, constants, and some operators operators.
[Constants](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constant.ini.html): Constants are named values, that never change.
[Constructor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/constructor.ini.html): The constructor is a magic method in a class, which is called at instantiation of an object, with the provided arguments.
[Container Orchestration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/container-orchestration.ini.html): Container orchestration is the automated management of the full lifecycle of containerised workloads across a cluster of machines:
[Container](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/container.ini.html): There are several usage of the word container:
[Continuation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/continuation.ini.html): A continuation represents the rest of a program's computation at a given point, reified as a callable value.
[Continue](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/continue.ini.html): ``continue`` is used within looping structures to skip the rest of the current loop iteration and resume the execution at the condition evaluation and then the beginning of the next iteration.
[Continuous Integration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/continuous-integration.ini.html): Continuous integration refers to the automation of the build and test phase of development.
[Design By Contract (DBC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/contract.ini.html): Design by Contract, or ``DbC``, is a software development approach that focuses on specifying the expected behavior of software components through preconditions, postconditions, and invariants.
[Contribution](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/contribution.ini.html): A contribution is a change submitted to a software project, typically in the form of a pull request or patch.
[Control Flow Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/control-flow-analysis.ini.html): Control flow analysis is a static analysis technique that examines the possible execution paths through a program.
[Control Flow Graph (CFG)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/control-flow-graph.ini.html): The Control Flow Graph is a logical representation of the execution of a source code.
[Control Flow](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/control-flow.ini.html): Control flow structures direct the way PHP executes the statements.
[Control Structures](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/control-structure.ini.html): Control structures are PHP statements that control the flow of execution.
[Controller](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/controller.ini.html): Controllers takes in charge one or several URL, extract data from the request, pass them to the model and finally, create and returns the view.
[Convention](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/convention.ini.html): A convention is a way in which something is usually done.
[Convert](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/convert.ini.html): To convert is to transform a piece of data, from one format to another.
[Cookie](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cookie.ini.html): Cookies serve as a fundamental web technology that enables websites to store small pieces of data directly within a user's web browser.
[Copy On Write (COW)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/copy-on-write.ini.html): ``Copy on write``, or COW, is a data management technique where data is shared between contexts, until it is actually modified.
[Core](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/core.ini.html): Core is the name of the main PHP engine part, which provides the base features of the language.
[Coroutine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/coroutine.ini.html): Coroutines are methods whose execution may be suspended and resumed.
[Correlation ID](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/correlation-id.ini.html): A correlation ID is a unique identifier attached to a request or event so that all related operations across multiple services or log entries can be linked together.
[count()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/count.ini.html): ``count()`` is a native PHP function, that returns the number of elements in the argument.
[Countable Interface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/countable.ini.html): The ``Countable`` interface is a native built-in interface that allows objects to be counted using the ``count()`` function.
[Coupling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/coupling.ini.html): Coupling refers to the degree of dependency between different classes of a software.
[Coverage](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/coverage.ini.html): The coverage is the concept that an operation has been applied to every part of a whole body.
[Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cqrs.ini.html): CQRS is an architectural pattern that separates read operations, Queries from write operations, Commands, into distinct models.
[Crack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/crack.ini.html): Cracking refers to breaking a security mechanism.
[Cyclic Redundancy Check 32-bit (CRC32)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/crc32.ini.html): ``CRC32`` stands for Cyclic Redundancy Check 32-bit.
[CRLF Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/crlf-injection.ini.html): CRLF injection is a security vulnerability that occurs when an attacker can insert a carriage-return, the ``\r`` char and line-feed, the ``\n`` char sequence into data that is later included in an HTTP response header, an email header, or another line-delimited protocol message.
[Cron](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cron.ini.html): A cron job is a scheduled task that runs PHP code automatically at specified times or intervals, without a web request.
[Cross-cutting Concerns](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cross-cutting-concerns.ini.html): Cross-cutting concerns are aspects of a system that affect multiple layers or components but cannot be cleanly encapsulated in any single module.
[Create, Read, Update And Delete (CRUD)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/crud.ini.html): CRUD stands for Create, Read, Update and Delete.
[To Crypt](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/crypt.ini.html): To crypt is to apply a data transformation that keeps the original information, but makes it impossible to read without decrypting.
[Cryptography](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cryptography.ini.html): Cryptography is the practice and study of techniques for secure communication in the presence of adversarial behavior.
[Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/csrf.ini.html): A ``CSRF``, or ``XSRF`` attack happens when a malicious website tricks a logged-in user's browser into sending an unwanted request to another application.
[CSS](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/css.ini.html): CSS, the Cascading Style Sheets, is the language used to describe the visual presentation of HTML documents.
[CSV Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/csv-injection.ini.html): A CSV injection is a vulnerability, where a csv file triggers the execution of a formula, stored in a cell.
[Comma Secparated Values (CSV)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/csv.ini.html): CSV is a data format, where values are separated by commas.
[Capture The Flag (CTF)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ctf.ini.html): Capture The Flag, abbreviated CTF, is a type of cybersecurity competition in which participants solve security challenges to find hidden strings called flags.
[Ctype](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ctype.ini.html): ``ctype`` is a set of functions used for character type checking.
[Client URL (CURL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/curl.ini.html): ``Curl`` is both a PHP extension and a multiprotocol file transfer library.
[Curly Brackets](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/curly-bracket.ini.html): Curly brackets have different usage:
[Currying](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/currying.ini.html): Currying is the transformation of a function that takes multiple arguments into a sequence of functions that each take a single argument.
[Custom Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/custom-function.ini.html): A custom function, or user-defined function, is a function declared by the developer, as opposed to a native PHP function provided by the engine or an extension.
[Custom](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/custom.ini.html): A feature is called ``custom`` when it is not part of the standard installation of PHP, any PHP extension or a publish library.
[Common Vulnerabilities And Exposures (CVE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cve.ini.html): CVE, or Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures, is a standardized, industry-recognized list of publicly disclosed cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures in software and hardware.
[Concurent Version System (CVS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cvs.ini.html): CSV is Concurrent Version System.
[Cyclomatic Complexity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/cyclomatic-complexity.ini.html): Cyclomatic complexity is a software metric of the complexity of a program.
[Dangling Reference](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dangling-reference.ini.html): In a foreach loop, a variable is used for looping through the array.
[Data Access Object (DAO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dao.ini.html): Data Access Object is a design pattern used to abstract and encapsulate all access to a data source, typically a database.
[Data Container](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data-container.ini.html): A data container is a structure that stores data.
[Data Flow Analysis (DFA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data-flow-analysis.ini.html): Data flow analysis is a static analysis technique that tracks how data values move through a program.
[Data Hiding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data-hiding.ini.html): Data hiding is a core principle of object-oriented programming that restricts direct access to certain components of an object.
[Data Leak](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data-leak.ini.html): A data leak is the publication of unwanted information, which leads to further security attack, or actual data theft.
[Data Mapper](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data-mapper.ini.html): A data mapper performs a bidirectional conversion of data, between PHP's OOP representation and a database storage.
[Data](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/data.ini.html): ``Data`` is a broad term that refers to information, facts, or statistics that are represented in a form suitable for processing, interpretation, or communication.
[Database Commit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/database-commit.ini.html): A database commit makes a transaction's changes permanent.
[Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/database.ini.html): A database is an organized set of data, which may be queried.
[Dataset](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dataset.ini.html): A dataset is an organized collection of data.
[Datastack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/datastack.ini.html): A data stack is a LIFO data structure:
[Data Structure](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/datastructure.ini.html): A data structure is a way of organizing and storing data in memory to enable efficient access and modification.
[Dates](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/date.ini.html): These functions allow to get the date and time from the server where the application is running.
[DateInterval](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dateinterval.ini.html): ``DateInterval`` is a class for the representation of period of time, or stretch of time.
[Datetime Trap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/datetime-trap.ini.html): When using ``Datetime`` objects, be aware that methods that return the modified date are actually returning the object itself.
[DateTime](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/datetime.ini.html): ``DateTime`` and ``DateTimeImmutable`` is a class for the representation of date and time.
[DateTimeInterface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/datetimeinterface.ini.html): ``DateTimeInterface`` is a PHP interface implemented by both ``DateTime`` and ``DateTimeImmutable``.
[Days Are Not 86400 Seconds](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/day-in-seconds.ini.html): Days in seconds is a classic bug when dealing with dates.
[Days In Month](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/days-in-month.ini.html): Days in month is a classic bug when dealing with dates.
[Database Abstraction Layer (DBA) Extension](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dba.ini.html): The Database Abstraction Layer, or DBA, extension provides a unified and consistent programming interface for interacting with various key-value database systems, especially those that follow the Berkeley DB-style architecture.
[Database Abstraction Layer (DBAL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dbal.ini.html): A Database Abstraction Layer, a DBAL, provides one unique interface to build, and execute queries on different databases.
[Domain Design Driven (DDD)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ddd.ini.html): Domain-Driven Design, aka DDD, is a software development approach that emphasizes structuring and modeling software based on the real-world domain it is meant to represent.
[Distributed Denial Of Service (DDOS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ddos.ini.html): Distributed Denial Of Service, or DDOS, is a type of attack where many computers flood a target, like a website or server, with traffic to overwhelm it and make it unavailable.
[Dead Code](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dead-code.ini.html): Dead code refers to sections of code that are no longer executed or reachable during the program's execution.
[Technical Debt](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/debt.ini.html): Debt generally refers to technical debt, as whole.
[Debug](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/debug.ini.html): Debugging is the action to diagnostic a bug, find its root cause, quick workaround and definitive fix for it.
[debug_backtrace()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/debug_backtrace.ini.html): ``debug_backtrace()`` is a PHP native function that generates a backtrace.
[Debugger](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/debugger.ini.html): A debugger is an extension or an external program that is used to help when searching for bugs or issues in source code.
[Declaration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/declaration.ini.html): A declaration is a statement that defines a structure, such as a function, a class, method, property, etc.
[Declarative Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/declarative-programming.ini.html): Declarative programming is a programming style, where the goal of the task is described, but not the method to reach it.
[declare Encoding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/declare-encoding.ini.html): ``declare()`` with the ``encoding`` keyword specifies the encoding of the file that holds PHP script.
[declare()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/declare.ini.html): ``declare()`` is a language construct.
[Decode](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/decode.ini.html): To decode is to transform a piece of data, from a specialized format to a human readable one.
[Decorator Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/decorator.ini.html): A decorator is a design pattern used to add behavior or responsibilities to objects dynamically, without altering their code.
[Decoupling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/decoupling.ini.html): Decoupling refers to the software engineering concept where different parts of a system are made as independent from each other as possible.
[To Decrypt](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/decrypt.ini.html): To decrypt is to apply a data transformation on encrypted data to access the original and readable information.
[Deduplication](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deduplication.ini.html): Deduplication is the process of detecting and discarding duplicate occurrences of an operation, message, or record.
[Deep Clone](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deep-clone.ini.html): A deep clone is an object cloning, where the cloned object also clone its own object properties.
[Default Parameter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/default-parameter.ini.html): Methods may define a default value for parameters.
[Default Value](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/default-value.ini.html): Default value is the value that a parameter or a property takes when it is used for the first time, and have not been provided.
[Default](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/default.ini.html): ``default`` is a PHP keyword, used with ``switch()`` and ``match()`` structures.
[default_charset](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/default_charset.ini.html): ``default_charset`` is the default value for the character encoding with ``htmlentities()``, ``html_entity_decode()`` and ``htmlspecialchars()``, when the encoding parameter is omitted.
[Defensive Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/defensive-programming.ini.html): Defensive programming is a programming approach where the code is written assuming that things can go wrong, even when they shouldn't, and it should actively guard against incorrect input, unexpected states, or misuse.
[Defer Statement](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/defer.ini.html): Go's ``defer`` statement schedules a function call to run when the enclosing function returns, regardless of which ``return`` statement was reached or whether a panic is in flight:
[define()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/define.ini.html): ``define()`` is a PHP function, that introduces global constant definitions.
[defined()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/defined.ini.html): ``defined()`` is a PHP function, that checks if a constant or a class constant is defined in the current context of execution.
[Definition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/definition.ini.html): A definition is the description of a particular constant, function, method, property, class, trait, interface or enum.
[delayedtargetvalidation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/delayedtargetvalidation.ini.html): The DelayedTargetValidation attribute is meant to make other PHP engine attributes optional.
[Delimiter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/delimiter.ini.html): Delimiters are specific characters that starts and ends a specific area.
[Dependency Injection Container](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dependency-injection-container.ini.html): A dependency injection container is a tool to make dependency injection easy to use.
[Dependency Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dependency-injection.ini.html): Dependency injection is a design pattern in which an object receives other objects that it depends on.
[Dependency Inversion (DIP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dependency-inversion.ini.html): The Dependency Inversion Principle, or DIP, is the D in SOLID.
[Dependency](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dependency.ini.html): A dependency refers to something that the current source code relies on to function correctly.
[Dependent Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dependent-type.ini.html): A dependent type is a type whose definition depends on a value.
[Deployment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deploy.ini.html): Deployment refers to the process of making a web application or website available for use on a server or hosting environment.
[Deprecated](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deprecated.ini.html): Deprecated is a native PHP attribute.
[Deprecation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deprecation.ini.html): Deprecation is the state of a feature which is still available, but not recommended anymore.
[Depth](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/depth.ini.html): Depth is a measure of how many an operation has been repeatedly applies, over and over.

[Dereferencing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dereferencing.ini.html): Dereferencing is the action to access a value, which is referenced with a pointer.
[Design Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/design-pattern.ini.html): A design pattern, or simply a pattern, is a general solution to a common problem.
[Desktop Application](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/desktop-app.ini.html): A desktop application runs natively on a user's local operating system and has direct access to the file system, hardware, and system APIs.
[Destructor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/destructor.ini.html): The destructor is a magic method in a class, which is called at shutdown of an object, in order to do any last minute cleaning.
[Determinism](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/determinism.ini.html): A deterministic function or algorithm always produces the same output for the same input, regardless of when or how many times it is called.
[Deterministic](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/deterministic.ini.html): A deterministic function is a function that produces the same output, based on the same input.
[DevOps](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/devops.ini.html): DevOps is a set of cultural practices, tools, and methodologies that combine software development, the Dev part and IT operations, the Ops part, to shorten the systems development lifecycle and deliver high-quality software continuously.
[Dictionary](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dictionary.ini.html): A dictionary is a data structure that holds a collection of values with a unique identifier.
[Diff](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/diff.ini.html): A diff is the textual difference between two versions of code.
[Digital Signature](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/digital-signature.ini.html): A digital signature is a method to ensure that a message was sent by a specific recipient.
[Dir](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/dir.ini.html): A directory, or dir, is a named collection of files on the file system.
[Direct Output](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/direct-output.ini.html): Direct output is the characteristics of a function or method to write data directly to the output stream.

[display_errors](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/display_errors.ini.html): ``display_errors`` configures the display of PHP errors, warnings and notices to the standard output.
[Distributed Monolith](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed-monolith.ini.html): A distributed monolith is an anti-pattern where a system is deployed as multiple separate services but remains tightly coupled in practice, changes to one service still require coordinated changes to others, defeating the purpose of the distribution.
[Distributed State](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed-state.ini.html): Distributed state refers to application state that is scattered across multiple, loosely-connected locations rather than being owned by a single, well-defined component.
[Distributed Systems](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed-systems.ini.html): A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appear to their users as a single coherent system.
[Distributed Tracing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed-tracing.ini.html): Distributed tracing tracks a single request as it flows through multiple services in a distributed system.
[Distributed Transactions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed-transactions.ini.html): A distributed transaction is a transaction that spans multiple independent data stores or services, requiring all participants to agree on the outcome (all commit or all abort).
[Distributed](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/distributed.ini.html): When something is distributed, it is spread across several autonomous instances, though it is reputed working as one.

STRING`` (Nowdoc syntax).

[Encapsulation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/encapsulation.ini.html): Encapsulation is the practice of bundling data, in properties, and methods that operate on that data into a single unit, a class, while restricting direct access to some of the object's components.

[Enumeration (enum)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/enum.ini.html): Enumerations are a restricting layer on top of classes and class constants, intended to provide a way to define a closed set of possible values for a type.
[.env File](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/env-file.ini.html): Environment files are a way to store and access environment variables, and store them in a file, not in the OS environment.
[Environment Variables](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/environment-variable.ini.html): Environment variables are a way to store and access configuration information or settings related to the runtime environment in which a PHP script is executed.
[Environment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/environment.ini.html): Environment variables are a way to store and access configuration information or settings related to the runtime environment in which a PHP script is executed.
[Eponymous](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/eponymous.ini.html): Eponymous means that it carries the same name.
[Equal Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/equal.ini.html): Equal is the state of two variables, which contains the same data, after an optional cast to a common type.
[Error Handler](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error-handler.ini.html): The PHP error handler is a method that manages errors when they arise, instead of the default PHP handler.
[Error Handling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error-handling.ini.html): Error handling is a broad concept that covers the different ways that PHP uses to signal that an error occurred.
[Error Reporting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error-reporting.ini.html): Error reporting is both a feature, a directive and a PHP native function.
[Error Suppression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error-suppression.ini.html): Error suppression is achieved with the ``@`` operator placed before an expression.
[Error](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error.ini.html): The ``Error`` class is a base class for all internal PHP error exceptions.
[Error Reporting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/error_reporting.ini.html): Error reporting is the process to report any issue while executing the code.
[Escape Character](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escape-character.ini.html): Escape characters are special characters, that introduce a special meaning, for example when redacting an escape sequence.
[Escape Data](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escape-data.ini.html): Escaping data is a method to write data in a neutral syntax for a target technology.
[HTML Escaping](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escape-html.ini.html): HTML escaping, also known as HTML encoding or character escaping, is a technique used to convert special characters in HTML markup into their corresponding HTML entities.
[Escape Sequences](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escape-sequence.ini.html): Escape sequences are sequences of characters with a special meaning.
[escapeshellarg()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escapeshellarg.ini.html): ``escapeshellarg()`` is a PHP function that wraps a string in single quotes and escapes any existing single quotes within it, making the string safe to pass as a single argument to a shell command.
[escapeshellcmd()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/escapeshellcmd.ini.html): ``escapeshellcmd()`` is a PHP function that escapes shell metacharacters in a full command string, preventing shell interpretation of characters such as ``&``, ``;``, ``|``, ``(``, ``)``, ``[``, ``]``, ``{``, ``}``, ``$``, ````, ``\``, ``"``, ``'``, and whitespace sequences that could alter the intended command.
[Eval()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/eval.ini.html): The ``eval()`` function executes a string as a PHP code.
[Event Driven](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-driven.ini.html): Event-driven programming is a programming paradigm that focuses on the flow of a program's execution being determined by events or actions that occur, rather than following a strictly sequential, procedural path.
[Event Loop](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-loop.ini.html): An event loop is a mechanism for handling asynchronous operations and event-driven programming.
[Event Replay](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-replay.ini.html): Event replay is the process of re-reading stored events from an event store to reconstruct the state of an aggregate, rebuild a projection, or bootstrap a new view of the data.
[Event Sourcing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-sourcing.ini.html): Event Sourcing is a pattern where instead of storing the current state of an entity, the sequence of events that led to that state is stored.
[Event Store](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-store.ini.html): An event store is an append-only database that stores domain events as the primary source of truth.
[Event Storming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/event-storming.ini.html): Event storming is a workshop, aiming at discovering the domain for an application.
