[Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mfa.ini.html): Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism that requires a user to provide two or more independent verification factors before access is granted.
[Micro-optimisation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/micro-optimisation.ini.html): A micro-optimisation is a piece of code that performs better than another, yet brings only a small gain of performance.
[Micro-second](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/micro-second.ini.html): Micro-seconds are one thousandth of a second.
[Micro-framework](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/microframework.ini.html): Small, minimal framework that provide only the core essentials for routing and handling HTTP requests, without large stacks or heavy dependencies.
[Microservice](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/microservice.ini.html): A microservice is a software architectural style that structures an application as a collection of small, independent services, each focused on performing a specific business functionality.
[microtime()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/microtime.ini.html): ``microtime()`` is a native PHP function, which returns the current timestamp, with the microseconds.
[Middleware Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/middleware.ini.html): A middleware is a design pattern used to extend the behavior of a component by chaining layers that can modify inputs, outputs, or short-circuit the call, dynamically at runtime.
[Migration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/migration.ini.html): Migration is the incremental upgrade of a piece of code.
[Minification](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/minification.ini.html): Minification is the process to remove elements that are useless in a PHP code, without changing its execution.
[Minus One -1](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/minus-one.ini.html): Minus one is the ``-1`` integer.
[Minus -](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/minus.ini.html): ``-`` is a native PHP operator, which allows to do subtraction on numbers, both integer and decimals, or produce the opposite of a number.
[Missing Validation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/missing-validation.ini.html): Missing Validation is a common security vulnerability that occurs when an application fails to validate user input or data before processing it.
[Mixed](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mixed.ini.html): A special type that represents any available type.
[Mixin](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mixin.ini.html): A mixin is a class-level construct that provides a set of methods intended to be incorporated into other classes, without forming a base-class relationship.
[Mobile Application](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mobile-app.ini.html): A mobile application runs on a mobile device such as a smartphone or tablet.
[Mock](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mock.ini.html): A mock replaces a code dependency with an object, to make testing easier.
[Model](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/model.ini.html): The model is the classes that describes the business logic.
[Modifier](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/modifier.ini.html): A modifier is a keyword that changes the behavior or properties of a class element, such as property, method, or constant.
[Module System with Explicit Exports](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/module-system.ini.html): A module system with explicit exports treats each source file, or each declared module, as a unit with a controlled boundary:
[Module](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/module.ini.html): In computer science, a module refers to a self-contained unit of code or software that encapsulates a specific functionality or set of related functionalities.
[Modulo](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/modulo.ini.html): Modulo is the mathematical operation:
[Monad](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/monad.ini.html): A monad is a design pattern from functional programming, formalized as a language construct in Haskell, that wraps a value in a context and defines two operations:
[MongoDB](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mongodb.ini.html): ``MongoDB`` is the official PIE extension that provides the low-level driver for connecting PHP to MongoDB, a document-oriented NoSQL database.
[Monitoring](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/monitoring.ini.html): Monitoring is the process of observing, tracking, and analyzing the performance, health, and behavior of applications or infrastructure over time.
[Monolith](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/monolith.ini.html): A monolith refers to an application that is built and deployed as one single, tightly integrated codebase.
[Monomorphization](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/monomorphization.ini.html): Monomorphization is a compile-time technique used to optimize generic or polymorphic code by generating separate, type-specific versions of a function or class for each concrete type it is used with.
[Move Semantics](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/move-semantics.ini.html): Move semantics, central to C++11 and Rust, let a value's underlying resources be transferred from a source object to a destination object instead of copied, when the source is about to be discarded anyway, such as a temporary or a variable passed by value into a function.
[Method Resolution Order (MRO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mro.ini.html): Method Resolution Order, or MRO, is the algorithm that determines the order in which base classes are searched when looking up a method or property in an inheritance hierarchy.
[Multi-byte](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multi-byte.ini.html): Multi-byte refers to string encoding where characters are coded over several bytes, rather than just one.
[Multi-pass Algorithm](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multi-pass-algorithm.ini.html): A multi-pass algorithm processes its input more than once, each pass building on information gathered, or output produced, by the previous one, instead of trying to solve the whole problem while reading the data only a single time, as a one-pass, or single-pass, algorithm would.
[Multidimensional Array](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multidimensional-array.ini.html): An array inside an array is called a multidimensional array.
[Multiple Dispatch](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multiple-dispatch.ini.html): Multiple dispatch, as found in Common Lisp (CLOS), Julia, and Dylan, selects which implementation of a function to run based on the runtime types of all of its arguments, not just the first one, the way ordinary object-oriented method calls do.
[Multiple Return Values](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multiple-return-value.ini.html): Some languages, such as Go and Lua, let a function return several values directly, separated by commas, without wrapping them in a container:
[Multiplication](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multiplication.ini.html): Multiplication is the mathematical multiplication.
[Multithreading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multithreading.ini.html): Multithreading refers to the ability of a PHP application to execute multiple threads, smaller units of a process, within the same process, allowing for concurrent execution of code.
[Multiton](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/multiton.ini.html): A multiton is a singleton which holds several instances of itself.
[Mutable State](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mutable-state.ini.html): Mutable state refers to data that can be changed after it is created.
[Mutation Testing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mutation-test.ini.html): Mutation testing is a way to enhance the quality of tests by checking how they react to a small perturbation of the code source.
[Mutex](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mutex.ini.html): A mutex, a mutual exclusion, is a synchronization primitive that prevents multiple processes or threads from accessing a shared resource simultaneously.
[Model - View - Controller (MVC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mvc.ini.html): MVC stands for Model - View - Controller.
[Minimum Viable Product (MVP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mvp.ini.html): Minimum Viable Product, or MVP, is the smallest version of a product that can be released to early adopters to gather feedback and validate assumptions with minimal effort.
[MySQL](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mysql.ini.html): ``mysql`` is both the name of a PHP extension that provides the interface to interact with the MySQL database, and the database itself.
[mysqli](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mysqli.ini.html): The mysqli extension provide access to MySQL database server, with version 4.
[mysqli_sql_exception](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/mysqli_sql_exception.ini.html): ``mysqli_sql_exception`` is thrown when a mysqli related error is found.
[N+1 Query Problem](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/n-query.ini.html): The ``N+1 query problem`` is a term used in the context of queries, and most often in the context of databases and Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) systems.
[Name Conventions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/name-convention.ini.html): A naming convention is a way to write a name for a structure, with a specific format.
[Name](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/name.ini.html): A name is a generic coding concept:
[Named Constructors](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/named-constructor.ini.html): Named constructor is a class organisation, where several static factories are created to instantiate the object, based on arguments.
[Named Parameters](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/named-parameter.ini.html): Named parameters, also known as named arguments allow to pass arguments to a function or method by specifying the parameter name along with the corresponding value, rather than relying on the order of the parameters.
[Named Return Values](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/named-return-value.ini.html): Go lets a function declare names for its return values as part of its signature, such as ``func divmod(a, b int) (q, r int)``.
[Named Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/named-type.ini.html): A named type is a type with a single name.
[Namespace Alias](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/namespace-alias.ini.html): PHP can alias, or import, constants, functions, classes, interfaces, traits, enumerations and namespaces.
[Namespace Name](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/namespace-name.ini.html): Namespaces names are strings of characters that can be used as namespaces names.
[Namespaces](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/namespace.ini.html): Namespaces are a way to encapsulate items like classes, interfaces, enumerations, traits, functions, and constants, preventing naming conflicts between different parts of a program.
[Namespaced Name](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/namespaced-name.ini.html): A namespaced name is a name that includes a ``\`` character.
[Naming Conflict](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/naming-conflict.ini.html): A naming conflict appears when two structures of the same type are using the same name.
[Naming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/naming.ini.html): Naming refers to the process of choosing appropriate names for variables, functions, classes, constants, traits, interfaces, enum, methods, parameters and any other programming elements.
[Not A Number (NAN)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nan.ini.html): Not A Number is a PHP constant, also called ``NAN``.
[PHP Natives](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/native-type.ini.html): PHP offers a selection of natives, which are not directly derived from a class, interface or enumeration name.
[Native](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/native.ini.html): A feature is called native or built-in when it is part of the standard installation of PHP.
[NativePHP](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nativephp.ini.html): NativePHP is a framework that allows PHP developers to build native desktop and mobile applications using the PHP language they already know.
[Needle](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/needle.ini.html): Needle is a classic name, used to designate the object of a search.
[negative-index](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/negative-index.ini.html): On PHP strings, a positive index starts at zero at the beginning of the string; and a negative index starts at the end of the string.
[Nested Attributes](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nested-attribute.ini.html): Arguments of attributes may be literals, such as integer or strings:
[Nesting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nesting.ini.html): Nesting refers to the practice of placing code block or construct inside another.
[Network](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/network.ini.html): A network is a collection of computers, linked together with communication capabilities.
[Neural Network](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/neural-network.ini.html): A neural network is a software made of many simple processing units, called neurons, connected and organized in layers.
[Neutral Element](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/neutral-element.ini.html): A neutral element is an piece of data which has no effect with a particular operation.
[Never Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/never.ini.html): ``never`` is a special return type that signals a method that never returns:
[New In Initializers](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/new-in-initializer.ini.html): It is possible to use a new expression for default values of static variables, parameter and constants.
[New Line](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/new-line.ini.html): A new-line ``\n`` character.
[New Without Parenthesis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/new-without-parenthesis.ini.html): The ``new`` operator may be called without using the parenthesis after the class name.
[new](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/new.ini.html): ``new`` creates a new object from a class.
[NoDiscard](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nodiscard.ini.html): ``NoDiscard`` attribute ensures that the returned value of a method is collected in the calling context.
[Nominal Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nominal-type.ini.html): Nominal typing is a type-compatibility rule under which two types are considered compatible only if they are explicitly declared as such, by name, through inheritance or interface implementation, regardless of whether they happen to share the same shape.
[Non-blocking](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/non-blocking.ini.html): Non-blocking I/O is a mode where read and write operations return immediately, even if no data is available or the buffer is full, instead of suspending execution until the operation can complete.
[Non Breakable Spaces](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/non-breakable-space.ini.html): Space is one of the base ASCII characters.
[Non-instantiable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/non-instantiable.ini.html): Usually, classes are turned into objects via instantiation.
[Non-numeric](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/non-numeric.ini.html): A non-numeric string is a string that can't be converted to a number without difficult choices.
[Non-static](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/non-static.ini.html): Non-static is the opposite of static, when used with methods, closures, properties or variables.
[Nonce](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nonce.ini.html): A nonce is a security token used to protect actions against unauthorized or malicious requests.
[Noop](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/noop.ini.html): A noop, or no operation, is a command that does nothing.
[@, No Scream Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/noscream.ini.html): The ``@`` operator prevent error reports in specific expressions.
[Nosql Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nosql-database.ini.html): A NoSQL database, often interpreted as ``not only SQL`` is a type of database that does not rely on the traditional relational model with tables, fixed schemas, and SQL as the primary query language.
[Not Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/not.ini.html): The not operator return the opposite boolean of a value.
[Notice](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/notice.ini.html): A notice is a non-critical runtime error that alerts developers about potential problems in the code, but doesn't stop script execution at that point.
[Nowdocs](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nowdoc.ini.html): Nowdocs is a string syntax, similar to heredoc, and without internal parsing.
[Null Byte](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/null-byte.ini.html): The null character, also called null byte, is a control character with the ASCII value ``0``.
[Null](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/null.ini.html): ``null`` is a special data type that represents a variable with no value or an undefined value.
[Nullable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nullable.ini.html): Nullable is the name of the NULL value, when used as a type.
[Null Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nullpattern.ini.html): The Null Pattern is a design pattern in software development that addresses the handling of null or undefined values in a more controlled and predictable manner.
[Null Safe Object Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nullsafe-object-operator.ini.html): The Null Safe Object Operator behaves like the object operator, until the object is null.
[Nullsafe](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/nullsafe.ini.html): A nullsafe operator is able to carry a function or fail graciously to ``null``.
[Number](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/number.ini.html): A number is either a float or an integer.
[Numeric Base](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/numeric-base.ini.html): The numeric base is the number of digits to be used when counting.
[Numeric Separator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/numeric-separator.ini.html): Since PHP 7.
[Numeric String](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/numeric-string.ini.html): A numeric string, is a string that contains a numeric value, or can be easily converted from string to numeric, integer or float.
[Numeronym](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/numeronym.ini.html): A numeronym is an abbreviation where a number replaces the omitted middle letters of a word, typically the count of letters skipped.
[OAuth](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/oauth.ini.html): OAuth is an open standard for access delegation, commonly used to grant applications access to user information on other services without exposing passwords.
[Obfuscation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/obfuscation.ini.html): Obfuscation refers to the practice of deliberately making something difficult to understand or obscure.
[Object API](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-api.ini.html): The Object API is a set of classes, interfaces, enums and traits to execute features.
[Object Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-injection.ini.html): Object injection happens when a string is deserialized to recreate an object, inside PHP, while the string is controlled by the attacker.
[Object Invasion](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-invasion.ini.html): PHP allows objects of one class to access private structures of an object of the same class.
[Object Lifecycle](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-lifecycle.ini.html): The lifecycle of an object is the sequence of stages an instance goes through, from its creation to its destruction.
[Object Nullsafe Operator ?->](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-nullsafe-operator.ini.html): The object nullsafe operator is an operator which gives access to properties and methods of an object.
[Object Operator ->](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-operator.ini.html): The Object Operator is the operator to access properties and methods from an object.
[Object Persistence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-persistence.ini.html): Object persistence is the feature that keeps an object available between two different PHP requests, on a web server.
[Object Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-syntax.ini.html): The object syntax is a coding style that involves using the object operator ``->``, and, to a lesser extend, the static operator ``:
[Object Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object-type.ini.html): ``object`` is a PHP type, which represents an object of any class, including anonymous classes.
[Object](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/object.ini.html): PHP includes a complete object model.
[Oblic Quotes](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/oblic-quote.ini.html): Oblic quotes, also called backticks, `` \` `` were used to make a shell call to the operating system.
[Observer Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/observer-pattern.ini.html): The Observer pattern defines a one-to-many dependency between objects:
[Observer Design Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/observer.ini.html): The observer is an architectural pattern, where objects register to get notified when events happen to a specific object.
[Open Closed Principle (OCP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ocp.ini.html): The open–closed principle states that classes should be open for extension, but closed for modification.
[Octal Integer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/octal-integer.ini.html): Integers can be specified in octal, or base 8, notation.
[Offensive Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/offensive-programming.ini.html): Offensive programming is a programming approach where the code is written code assuming that internal states should never go wrong, and don't have to be guarded against.
[Offset](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/offset.ini.html): The term offset refers to the position or index of an element within an array or a string.
[On Premise Software](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/on-premise-software.ini.html): On-premise software, sometimes abbreviated as ``on‑prem``, refers to software that is installed and runs on computers and servers within the physical premises of the organization that uses it, rather than on remote servers in a cloud provider's data center.
[One Liner](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/one-liner.ini.html): A one-liner refers to a concise and often single expression that accomplishes a specific task.
[One-shot](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/one-shot.ini.html): One-shot is an I/O watcher mode where the handler fires exactly once and is automatically deregistered after the first event.
[one](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/one.ini.html): One is the ``1`` integer.
[Onion Architecture](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/onion-architecture.ini.html): Onion Architecture is a software architecture pattern introduced by ``Jeffrey Palermo``.
[OOP (Object Oriented Programming)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/oop.ini.html): Procedural Programming is a programming paradigm where a program is structured as a series of step-by-step instructions, using procedures, or functions, and control flow statements like loops, conditionals, and function calls.
[OPcache Preloading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opcache-preloading.ini.html): OPcache preloading, introduced in version 7.
[Opcache](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opcache.ini.html): OPcache improves PHP performance by storing precompiled script bytecode in shared memory, thereby removing the need for PHP to load and parse scripts on each request.
[Opcode Specialisation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opcode-specialization.ini.html): Opcode specialization refers to the process of optimizing how PHP's Zend Engine executes opcodes, by tailoring them to specific contexts, data types, or execution paths.
[Opcode](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opcode.ini.html): Opcodes, short for ``operation codes``, refer to low-level instructions that the PHP interpreter uses to execute PHP scripts.
[Open Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/open-class.ini.html): Open classes allow external code to add or replace methods on an existing class after it has been defined, including built-in or third-party classes.
[Open Source](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/open-source.ini.html): Open source means that the source code of software is publicly available, and people are allowed to view, use, modify, and share it under a specific license.
[Open Tag](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/open-tag.ini.html): PHP commands are placed between the PHP tags:
[open_basedir](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/open_basedir.ini.html): The ``open_basedir`` directive limits access to files in a list of provided directories, and their nested directories.
[OpenSSL](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/openssl.ini.html): The OpenSSL Project develops and maintains the OpenSSL software - a robust, commercial-grade, full-featured toolkit for general-purpose cryptography and secure communication.
[OpenSwoole](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/openswoole.ini.html): ``OpenSwoole`` is a PIE extension providing an event-driven, asynchronous, coroutine-based programming framework for PHP.
[OpenTelemetry](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opentelemetry.ini.html): ``OpenTelemetry`` is a PIE extension that provides the low-level instrumentation hooks needed to generate distributed traces, metrics, and logs from PHP applications, following the vendor-neutral OpenTelemetry standard.
[Operand](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/operand.ini.html): An operand is an argument for an operator.
[Operator Overloading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/operator-overloading.ini.html): Operator overloading is the feature that creates custom usage of operators.
[Operator Precedence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/operator-precedence.ini.html): The operator precedence dictates in which order the operators in a same sequence are executed.
[Operators](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/operator.ini.html): An operator takes one or more values, or expressions in programming jargon, and yields another value, so that the construction itself becomes an expression.
[Opinionated](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/opinionated.ini.html): A tool, library, or framework is called opinionated when it enforces a specific way of solving a problem:
[Optimisation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/optimisation.ini.html): Optimisation is the act of refactoring code to reduce the amount of resources used during execution.
[Option Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/option-type.ini.html): An option type, called ``Option`` in Rust and OCaml or ``Maybe`` in Haskell, is a native sum type with exactly two cases:
[Option](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/option.ini.html): An option represents a value that may or may not be present.
[Optional Parameter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/optional-parameter.ini.html): Optional parameter are parameter fitted with a default value.
[Oracle](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/oracle.ini.html): Oracle is a relational database management system developed by Oracle Corporation.
[Order Of Execution](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/order-of-execution.ini.html): The order of execution is the sequence that PHP uses to run the code.
[Object Relational Mapping (ORM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/orm.ini.html): Object Relational Mapping is a tool that bridges the OOP world to databases:
[One-Time Password (OTP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/otp.ini.html): A One-Time Password, the OTP, is a password that is valid for only one authentication session or transaction.
[OuterIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/outeriterator.ini.html): ``OuterIterator`` is a PHP interface that extends ``Iterator`` to represent iterators that wrap other iterators.
[Outgoing Data](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/outgoing-data.ini.html): Outgoing data are data submitted by PHP to an external user.
[OutOfRangeException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/outofrangeexception.ini.html): ``OutOfRangeException`` is a built-in exception class that is used to indicate that an index or value is outside the acceptable range.
[Output Buffering](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/output-buffering.ini.html): Output buffering, also abbreviated ``ob``, is a mechanism that captures output generated by the application, instead of sending it directly to the browser.
[Over-engineer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overengineer.ini.html): Over-engineering refers to the practice of designing and implementing a solution to a problem that goes beyond what is necessary or exceeds the requirements of the task.
[Overflow](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overflow.ini.html): Overflow happens when a value goes beyond a limit:
[OverflowException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overflowexception.ini.html): The ``OverflowException`` is the exception thrown when adding an element to a full container.
[Overloading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overloading.ini.html): Overloading provides means to dynamically create properties and methods.
[Override Attribute](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/override.ini.html): This is a native PHP attribute, which tells the engine that a method must be overriding the same method in a parent.
[Overriding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overriding.ini.html): Overriding is an OOP concept where a child class provides its own implementation of a method that already exists in a parent class.
[Overwrite](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/overwrite.ini.html): Overwriting is the process to replace an existing structure, with another one, entirely different.
[OWASP](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/owasp.ini.html): OWASP stands for Open Web Application Security Project.
[PaaS](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/paas.ini.html): PaaS, or Platform as a Service, is a cloud-computing model in which a provider manages the underlying infrastructure, like hardware, operating system, and runtime, so that developers can focus on deploying and running applications without administering servers.
[pack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pack.ini.html): ``pack()`` is a native PHP function which converts given arguments into a binary string according to format.
[Package Manager](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/package-manager.ini.html): A package manager is a tool that automates the process of installing, upgrading, configuring, and removing software packages.
[Package-Level](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/package-visibility.ini.html): Package-level, or internal, visibility is an access modifier that sits between ``public`` and ``private``:
[Packagist](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/packagist.ini.html): Packagist is the default package repository for PHP.
[Pagination](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pagination.ini.html): Pagination is a technique used in web development to break down and display a large set of data or content into smaller, more manageable chunks called pages.
[Pair](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pair.ini.html): A pair is a data structure that holds exactly two related values, often a key and a value, or two correlated items.
[Paradigm](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/paradigm.ini.html): A programming paradigm is basically a style or way of thinking about how the code is written.
[Parallel](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parallel.ini.html): Running processes in parallel means that several distinct processes are running at the same time.
[Parameter Removal](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parameter-removal.ini.html): Parameter removal is the operation to remove a parameter in a method's signature.
[Parameter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parameter.ini.html): A parameter is a variable in a method definition.
[parent](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parent.ini.html): ``parent`` is one of the three special keywords that are used to access properties or methods from inside the class definition.
[Parenthesis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parenthesis.ini.html): Parenthesis are the ``(`` and ``)``.
[Parquet](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parquet.ini.html): Parquet an open-source, column-oriented data file format designed for efficient data storage and high-performance analytics.
[Parse](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parse.ini.html): To parse, or parsing, refers to the process of analyzing and interpreting the structure of a data to extract meaningful information.
[parse_str()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parse_str.ini.html): ``parse_str()`` parses a query string and extracts its components, name and value, in an array.
[parse_url()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parse_url.ini.html): ``parse_url()`` parses a URL string and returns its components as an associative array.
[ParseError](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parseerror.ini.html): A ``ParseError`` is thrown when an error occurs while parsing PHP code, such as when ``eval()`` is called or when including non-compilable code.
[Parser](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/parser.ini.html): The PHP parser is a piece of software that process a piece of text and extract tokens, with their PHP meanings.
[Partial Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/partial-function.ini.html): A partial function is a function that has some parameters already known, and still needs some final ones before execution.
[Pascal Case](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pascal-case.ini.html): Pascal case, also written ``PascalCase`` is a way of writing names without spaces, where:
[Passphrase](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/passphrase.ini.html): A passphrase is a longer and more critical password.
[Password API](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/password-ext.ini.html): The Password API, or extension, is a part of the Core of PHP, which provides an easy to use wrapper around ``crypt()`` and some other password hashing algorithms, to make it easy to create and manage passwords in a secure manner.
[Password](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/password.ini.html): A password is a secret or confidential string of characters that is used for authentication and access control to verify the identity of a user.
[Path Traversal](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/path-traversal.ini.html): A path traversal vulnerability is a security flaw that allows an attacker to access files or directories outside the intended location on a server.
[Path](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/path.ini.html): A path is a representation of the location of a file or directory within the directory structure.
[Pattern-Based Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pattern-based-analysis.ini.html): Pattern-based analysis is a static analysis technique that detects issues by matching source code against a library of known patterns, without building a full model of the program's execution.
[Pattern Matching](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pattern-matching.ini.html): Pattern Matching as a language concept contains two parts:
[Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pattern.ini.html): A pattern is a named, reusable solution to a commonly recurring problem in software design.
[Pay-as-you-go](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pay-as-you-go.ini.html): Pay-as-you-go is a cloud billing model in which customers are charged only for the resources they actually consume, rather than paying a fixed fee for reserved capacity.
[Payload](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/payload.ini.html): A payload is the data carried by a request or response, distinct from its envelope:
[Process Control (pcntl)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pcntl.ini.html): Process Control is a set of native PHP function, that handles signals coming from the OS.
[pcntl_signal()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pcntl_signal.ini.html): ``pcntl_signal()`` installs a signal handler for a given OS signal.
[PCOV](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pcov.ini.html): ``PCOV`` is a PIE extension dedicated to code coverage collection.
[Perl Compatible Regular Expressions (PCRE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pcre.ini.html): PCRE is the library that supports regular expressions.
[PDF](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pdf.ini.html): PDF, Portable Document Format, is a file format developed by Adobe that presents documents independently of software, hardware, or operating system.
[PHP Data Objects (PDO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pdo.ini.html): PHP Data Objects, aka PDO, is an interface that abstract access to databases.
[PDOException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pdoexception.ini.html): ``PDOException`` represents an error raised by PDO.
[Product Requirements Document (PDR)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pdr.ini.html): A Product Requirements Document, or PRD, is a formal document used in product management to outline the purpose, features, functionality, and behavior of a product or feature before development begins.
[PEAR](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pear.ini.html): PEAR stands for 'PHP Extension and Application Repository'.
[PHP Extension C Library (PECL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pecl.ini.html): PECL is a repository for PHP Extensions, providing a directory of all known extensions and hosting facilities for downloading and development of PHP extensions.
[Penetration Test](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pentest.ini.html): A pentest, short for penetration test, is a simulated cyberattack against a server, or application to identify and exploit security vulnerabilities.
[Performance](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/performance.ini.html): Performance refers to the speed of execution, or, to a lesser extent, to the economy of resources used.
[Permission](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/permission.ini.html): A permission is the approval or consent given to someone or something to perform a specific action, access certain information, or use a resource.
[Persistence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/persistence.ini.html): Persistence is related to the life cycle of a resource.
[PostgreSQL](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pgsql.ini.html): ``pgsql`` is the name of the PHP extension that provides the interface to interact with the PostgreSQL database.
[Phantom Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phantom-type.ini.html): A phantom type is a generic type that carries a type parameter which does not appear in the runtime representation of the value.
[Phar](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phar.ini.html): Phar is a the PHP Archive.
[PharException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pharexception.ini.html): The PharException class is a phar-specific exception.
[PHP AI Editor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-ai-editor.ini.html): A PHP AI editor is a development environment or plugin that integrates artificial intelligence to assist with writing, reviewing, and refactoring PHP code.
[PHP Native Attributes](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-attribute.ini.html): PHP defines its own native attributes.
[PHP Constants](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-constant.ini.html): PHP provides a set of predefined global constants.
[PHP Docker Container](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-docker-container.ini.html): This is a Docker container, with a pre-installed PHP executable.
[PHP Editor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-editor.ini.html): A PHP editor is a software tool used to write, edit, and debug PHP source code.
[PHP Extensions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-extension.ini.html): Extensions are PHP engine module system.
[Framework Interoperability Group (FIG)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-fig.ini.html): ``FIG`` is the Framework Interoperability Group.

[PHP Foundation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-foundation.ini.html): The PHP Foundation focuses on providing financial support and guidance to PHP language developers to support its goals of improving the language for its users, providing high-quality maintenance, and improving the PHP language project to retain current contributors and to integrate new contributors.
[PHP Native Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-function.ini.html): PHP defines its own native functions.

[PHP Tags](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/php-tag.ini.html): When PHP parses a file, it looks for opening and closing tags, which are <?php and ?> which tell PHP to start and stop interpreting the code between them.

[PHPdoc](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phpdoc.ini.html): PHPDoc is a style of PHP comments.
[phpinfo()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phpinfo.ini.html): ``phpinfo()`` is one of the most famous PHP function.
[Phptoken](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phptoken.ini.html): ``PHPToken`` is a class, part of the Tokenizer extension, that provides an object-oriented interface for working with tokens generated by PHP's tokenizer, used for parsing PHP code into tokens.
[PHPunit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/phpunit.ini.html): PHPUnit is an open-source testing framework for the PHP programming language.
[PHP Installer For Extensions (PIE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pie.ini.html): PIE is an extension installer for PHP, designed as a successor to PECL and developed by the PHP Foundation.
[Pipe Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pipe-operator.ini.html): The pipe operator is ``|>``.
[Pipe Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pipe.ini.html): The ``pipe`` operator is the ``|>`` operator.
[Pipeline](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pipeline.ini.html): A pipeline is a chain of steps that composes a sequential set of actions.
[Plain Text](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/plain-text.ini.html): Plain Text refers to data that is human-readable, and not protected by any cryptography transformation.
[Plugin](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/plugin.ini.html): A plugin is a component that add new features to an existing code.
[Plus +](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/plus.ini.html): ``+`` is a native PHP operator, which has three usages:
[PNG](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/png.ini.html): PNG, the Portable Network Graphics format, is a lossless raster image format that supports transparency through an alpha channel.
[Pointer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pointer.ini.html): Pointers are a fundamental concept in programming, especially in languages like C and C++.
[Poisoned Pipeline Execution (PPE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/poisoned-pipeline-execution.ini.html): Poisoned Pipeline Execution, or PPE, is a CI/CD security attack in which an attacker injects malicious code into a repository's CI/CD pipeline configuration or triggered workflow, causing the pipeline to execute attacker-controlled code in a trusted environment.
[Polling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/polling.ini.html): Polling is a technique where a script repeatedly checks the status of a resource or condition at regular intervals, until a result is available or a timeout is reached.
[Polyfill](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/polyfill.ini.html): A polyfill is a piece of code that provides modern functionality on older browsers or environments that lack support for certain features.
[Polymorphism](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/polymorphism.ini.html): Polymorphism is a fundamental concept in object-oriented programming, or OOP.
[Pop](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pop.ini.html): Pop is the operation of removing and returning the last element of a stack or array.
[Plain Old PHP Object (POPO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/popo.ini.html): ``Plain Old PHP Object``, or POPO for shorts, refers to a simple PHP class that does not depend on any framework, library, or special inheritance.
[Port](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/port.ini.html): In computer networking, a port is a communication endpoint that facilitates the transfer of data between different processes or devices over a network.
[Portability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/portability.ini.html): Portability refers to the ability of a PHP codebase to run on different platforms or environments without requiring significant modifications.
[Ports And Adapters](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ports-and-adapters.ini.html): Ports and Adapters is the formal name for the pattern underlying hexagonal architecture, coined by Alistair Cockburn.
[Positional Parameters](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/positional-parameter.ini.html): Way of calling a function or method, where argument which are identified by their position in the signature or the call.
[Positive Integer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/positive-integer.ini.html): A positive integer is an integer value which is 0 or larger.
[Post-increment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/post-increment.ini.html): Post-increment is the ``++`` operator, used on a variable, when it is placed after the variable.

[Pull Request (PR)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pr.ini.html): A Pull Request is a feature of version control systems, vcs, that allows a developer to propose changes to a codebase.
[Pre-increment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pre-increment.ini.html): Pre-increment is the ``++`` operator, used on a variable, when it is placed before the variable.
[Precedence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/precedence.ini.html): Precedence organizes the way operators are executed, when they are mixed in the same expression.
[precision](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/precision.ini.html): Precision refers to the number of digits are used to represent and display floating-point numbers.
[Predefined Constants](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/predefined-constant.ini.html): Predefined constants are PHP native constants, available at the start of the PHP engine.
[PHP Predefined Exception](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/predefined-exception.ini.html): The predefined exceptions are the exceptions that are built-in the PHP engine.
[Prefix](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/prefix.ini.html): A prefix is a word extension, placed before the name.
[Preg_match](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/preg_match.ini.html): ``preg_match()`` searches a string for a match to the regular expression given in pattern.
[preg_split()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/preg_split.ini.html): ``preg_split()`` splits strings with a regular expression.
[Premature Optimization](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/premature-optimization.ini.html): Premature optimization is the practice of optimizing code before profiling has identified it as a bottleneck.
[Prepared Query](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/prepared-query.ini.html): A prepared query is a compiled query, where data are provided as parameters.
[Array Prepend](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/prepend.ini.html): Prepend is an array operation, which adds an element at the beginning of the array.
[Preprocessing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/preprocess.ini.html): Preprocessing is an optimisation technique, where a value is calculated even before the execution of a script.
[Primitive Obsession](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/primitive-obsession.ini.html): Primitive Obsession a bias in the way the code is written.
[Print](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/print.ini.html): ``print()`` is a PHP language construct used to output text to the browser or the output stream.
[print_r()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/print_r.ini.html): ``print_r()`` is a built-in PHP function used for displaying the contents of an array or an object in a human-readable format.
[printf()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/printf.ini.html): ``printf()`` is a PHP native function, that places the content of variables into strings, with the help of placeholders and formatting commands.
[Privacy](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/privacy.ini.html): Privacy in software refers to the protection of personal and sensitive user data.
[Private Visibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/private.ini.html): The private keyword is part of the three keywords to define visibility of a method, property or class constant.
[Procedural Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/procedural-programming.ini.html): Procedural Programming is a programming paradigm where a program is structured as a series of step-by-step instructions, using procedures, or functions, and control flow statements like loops, conditionals, and function calls.
[Process](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/process.ini.html): A process is an instance of a running program managed by the operating system.
[Production](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/production.ini.html): In web development and IT infrastructure, the term production refers to the environment or set of servers where the final, fully functional version of an application is deployed and actively running for real users or a live audience.
[PHP Profiler](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/profiler.ini.html): A profiler is an application that helps track execution performances, and spots slow parts of the application for optimization.
[Profiling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/profiling.ini.html): Profiling is the process of measuring the performance of a program to identify bottlenecks and areas for optimization.
[Program To Interface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/program-to-interface.ini.html): ``Program to an interface`` means the code should depend on abstractions, not concrete implementations.
[Promise](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/promise.ini.html): A promise is a programming construct used in asynchronous programming to handle the results or errors of an asynchronous operation that may not have completed yet.
[Promoted Properties](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/promoted-property.ini.html): Promoted properties are a class's arguments, which are declared as properties and automatically assigned their value at instantiation.
[Propagation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/propagation.ini.html): Propagation is the consequence of one piece of code, that applies to another part of the code, via a dependency.
[Property Hook](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/property-hook.ini.html): Property hooks are a feature of PHP 8.
[Properties](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/property.ini.html): Class properties are variables, local to an object or a class.
[Proprietary Source](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/proprietary-source.ini.html): Proprietary source code is software whose source code is not publicly available and is protected by copyright and licensing restrictions.
[Protected Visibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/protected.ini.html): The ``protected`` keyword is part of the three keywords to define visibility of a method, property or constant.
[Protocol Buffers](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/protobuf.ini.html): ``protobuf`` is a PIE extension implementing Protocol Buffers, Google's language-neutral, binary serialization format.
[Protocol](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/protocol.ini.html): A protocol is a scheme part of a URL, with an associated wrapper.
[Proxy Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/proxy-class.ini.html): A proxy class is a class that stands in for another class, intercepting calls and delegating them to the real object.
[Proxy](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/proxy.ini.html): A proxy class is a structural design pattern that provides a substitute or placeholder for another object.
[Pseudo-type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pseudo-type.ini.html): A pseudo-type is a keyword and a type that describes an input or output, but is not an actual type.
[Pseudo-variable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pseudo-variable.ini.html): A pseudo-variable is a term used to describe a variable that looks like a variable but does not represent one.
[PHP Standards Recommendations (PSR)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/psr.ini.html): ``PSR`` stands for PHP Standards Recommendations.
[Public Visibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/public.ini.html): The ``public`` keyword is part of the three keywords to define visibility of a method, property or constant.
[Pub/Sub](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/pubsub.ini.html): Publish/Subscribe, or pubsub for short, is a messaging pattern in which message producers, the publishers, send messages to named channels or topics without knowing who will receive them, and message consumers, the subscribers, register interest in topics and receive only the messages published to those topics.

[Quality](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/quality.ini.html): Code quality describes how well software code meets the goals of being correct, readable, maintainable, efficient, and reliable over time.
[Query String](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/query-string.ini.html): The query string is a part of a URL that contains data and parameters to be passed to a web server.
[Query](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/query.ini.html): Query is a generic term.
