[Quine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/quine.ini.html): A quine is a piece of code that produces itself.
[Quoted Printable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/quoted-printable.ini.html): Quoted-printable is a content transfer encoding defined in RFC 2045 that's primarily used in email to ensure that message data remains intact when transmitted over protocols that are not 8-bit clean, such as SMTP.
[Race Condition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/race-condition.ini.html): A Race Condition is a broad term for any situation where the outcome of a program depends on the timing of uncontrollable events.
[RACI Model](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/raci.ini.html): RACI model is an accountability model, which list 

+ Responsible:
[Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rag.ini.html): Retrieval Augmented Geenration, aka RAG, is a technique used in artificial intelligence, especially in large language models, to improve the accuracy and relevance of generated responses.
[Railroad Programming](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/railroad-programming.ini.html): Railroad programming is a functional programming paradigm that manages flow control, especially success vs failure, without messy if/else chains or exceptions everywhere.
[Rainbow Table](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rainbow-table.ini.html): A rainbow table is a precomputed table used to reverse cryptographic hash functions, mainly to recover plaintext passwords from their hash.
[Random Access](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/random-access.ini.html): Random access is the ability to read or write any element of a data structure, or any byte of a file, directly, in constant time, without having to traverse the elements that precede it.
[Random](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/random.ini.html): Random generators generates numbers or strings.
[Random\CryptoSafeEngine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/random_cryptosafeengine.ini.html): ``Random\CryptoSafeEngine`` is a marker interface ``Random`` extension.
[Random\Engine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/random_engine.ini.html): ``Random\Engine`` is the base interface for all random number generator engines introduced in version 8.
[Range Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/range-type.ini.html): A first-class range type represents an interval of values, such as ``1.
[range()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/range.ini.html): ``range()`` produces an array with all values between the two arguments of the function.
[RangeException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rangeexception.ini.html): The RangeException exception is thrown if a value is out of range.
[Rate Limiting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rate-limit.ini.html): Rate limiting is a technique that controls the frequency of requests a client can make to a service within a given time window.
[Role Based Access Control (RBAC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rbac.ini.html): RBAC, or Role-Based Access Control, is a security model used to restrict system access based on user roles.
[Remote Code Execution (RCE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rce.ini.html): It is a type of security vulnerability where an attacker can remotely execute arbitrary code on a target server or system.
[Relational DataBase Management System (RDBMS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rdbms.ini.html): RDBMS stands for Relational DataBase Management System.
[RDF Triple Store](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rdf-triple-store.ini.html): An RDF triple store is a database purpose-built to store and query RDF, Resource Description Framework, data:
[RdKafka](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rdkafka.ini.html): ``rdkafka`` is a PIE extension providing a PHP client for Apache Kafka, a distributed event-streaming platform.
[ReactPHP](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reactphp.ini.html): ReactPHP is a low-level library for event-driven programming.
[Readability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/readability.ini.html): A code is readable, or easy to read, if all the needed information is available in the code, without the need to reach for an extra piece of reference to understand it.
[Readable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/readable.ini.html): Something is readable when its current value may be accessed.
[Reading](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reading.ini.html): Reading is the action to access a value in a data container, to make use of it.
[Readline](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/readline.ini.html): ``readline`` is the PHP extension to the GNU Readline library.
[Readonly](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/readonly.ini.html): A property or a class can be declared readonly, which prevents modification of the property or all properties after initialization.
[Real Time](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/real-time.ini.html): Real time generally refers to the ability of an application to process data with minimal delay or in a continuous manner, often appearing instantaneous to the user.
[Real Numbers](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/real.ini.html): Real numbers are represented using the ``float`` data type.
[Record Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/record-type.ini.html): A record type, also called a data class, is a construct dedicated to grouping named fields into an immutable value, with equality, hashing, and a string representation generated automatically from the field list, rather than written by hand.
[Rector](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rector.ini.html): Rector is a PHP refactoring tool that automates code upgrades and migrations.
[Recursion](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursion.ini.html): Recursion is a method or a function which calls itself.
[Recursive Array](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursive-array.ini.html): A recursive array is an array that contains a reference to itself.
[RecursiveArrayIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursivearrayiterator.ini.html): ``RecursiveArrayIterator`` extends ``ArrayIterator`` to allow recursive iteration over nested arrays and objects.
[RecursiveDirectoryIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursivedirectoryiterator.ini.html): ``RecursiveDirectoryIterator`` extends ``FilesystemIterator`` to recursively traverse an entire directory tree.
[RecursiveIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursiveiterator.ini.html): The ``RecursiveIterator`` interface is an interface that is able to iterate through nested iterators.
[RecursiveTreeIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/recursivetreeiterator.ini.html): The ``RecursiveTreeIterator`` allows iterating over a ``RecursiveIterator`` to generate an ASCII graphic tree.
[Red-Green-Refactor](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/red-green-refactor.ini.html): Red-Green-Refactor is the core cycle of Test-Driven Development, TDD, a software development methodology where tests are written before the implementation code.
[Redirect](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/redirect.ini.html): An HTTP redirect instructs the client to navigate to a different URL.
[Refactoring](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/refactoring.ini.html): Refactoring, as in any programming language, refers to the process of restructuring and improving the codebase of an application without changing its external behavior.
[Reference Count](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reference-count.ini.html): Reference counting is the way PHP keeps track of the usage of a value.
[References](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reference.ini.html): References allow creating multiple variables that refer to the same underlying value in memory.
[Refinement Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/refinement-type.ini.html): A refinement type is a base type combined with a logical predicate that every value of that type must satisfy.
[Reflection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reflection.ini.html): PHP reflection is a feature that allows to inspect and interact with the structure of classes, interfaces, methods, properties, and other elements of the code, during runtime.
[ReflectionException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reflectionexception.ini.html): ReflectionException is thrown whenever the Reflection API encounters an error.
[Reflector](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reflector.ini.html): ``Reflector`` is the base interface that all PHP Reflection classes implement.
[Regular Expression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/regex.ini.html): A regular expression, or regex, is a sequence of characters that specifies a search pattern.
[Regexploit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/regexploit.ini.html): A regexploit is a poorly build regex, which takes a long time to execute, even for small inputs.
[Register Globals](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/register-globals.ini.html): Register globals is an old and removed feature of PHP, which automatically created variables from the incoming super globals, such as $_GET, $_POST, $_REQUEST, $_COOKIES.
[register_argc_argv](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/register_argc_argv.ini.html): This directive controls the availability of the ``$argv`` and ``$argc`` PHP native variables.
[Registry](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/registry.ini.html): A registry may be the following things:
[Regression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/regression.ini.html): A regression is a return to a previous, less developed state, typically a bug that was once fixed but has reappeared.
[Relative Name](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relative-name.ini.html): A relative name is a name that is completed by the current namespace to lead to the definition of the referenced structure.
[Relative Types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relative-types.ini.html): Relative types are types that are not explicit, but relative to the current class.
[Relative](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relative.ini.html): A ``relative`` value is dependent on the context in which it operates.
[Relaxed Comparison](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relaxed-comparison.ini.html): Relaxed comparison, also known as loose comparison, is a comparison style that allows for non-strict equality checks between values.
[Relaxed Keywords](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relaxed-keywords.ini.html): The relaxed keywords is the feature that allows most PHP keywords to be used as a method or class constant name.
[Relaxed Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relaxed-syntax.ini.html): Relaxed syntax refers to the extra freedom in naming class elements, such as constant and methods, compared to creating global constant and functions.
[Relay Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/relay-function.ini.html): A relay function, or a relay method, is a function that calls another function, with the same arguments.
[Release](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/release.ini.html): A release is a final version of a software.
[Reliability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reliability.ini.html): Reliability is the ability of a system to perform its intended function consistently and without failure over a given period.
[Render](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/render.ini.html): To render, or renderingm is the action to take a template and its related data, to produce a target format.
[Renderer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/renderer.ini.html): A renderer is an piece of code that applies the rules to produce a target format.
[Read Eval Print Loop (REPL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/repl.ini.html): PHP's REPL mode, or Read-Eval-Print Loop, is an interactive shell that allows to execute PHP code line by line and see immediate results.
[Replay](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/replay.ini.html): A replay is the act of returning a previously stored result to a repeated request without re-executing the underlying operation.
[Request-Response](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/request-response.ini.html): Request-response is a communication pattern where a caller, the client, sends a request and synchronously waits for the callee, the server, to return a response before continuing.
[Request](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/request.ini.html): A request is an object that encapsulates an incoming HTTP message:
[Reserved Names](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reserved-name.ini.html): PHP shares several namespaces with the custom code, and has reserved some names for its own usage.
[Resilience](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/resilience.ini.html): Resilience is the ability of a system to absorb failures and disturbances and continue providing acceptable service, or recover quickly when a failure does occur.
[Resource Leak](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/resource-leak.ini.html): A resource leak occurs when a resource, such as a file handle, database connection, network socket, or stream, is opened but never properly closed.
[resource](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/resource.ini.html): A resource is a special type of data, holding a reference to an external resource.
[Responsability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/responsability.ini.html): Responsibility refers to a reason to change or a goal to achieve.
[Response](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/response.ini.html): A response is the dataset returned by a server, to a query.
[REST API](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rest-api.ini.html): REST stands for Representational state transfer.
[Result Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/result-type.ini.html): A result type is a built-in sum type, such as Rust's ``Result<T, E>`` or Swift's ``Result<Success, Failure>``, that represents either a successful outcome carrying a value, or a failure carrying an error.
[Retry](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/retry.ini.html): A retry is the act of re-attempting an operation that previously failed, typically due to a transient error such as a network timeout, a temporarily unavailable service, or a rate-limit response.
[Return Carriage](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/return-carriage.ini.html): A return carriage is a whitespace character, that historically represents moving the cursor to the beginning of the line, without advancing to the next line.
[Return Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/return-type.ini.html): Return types are the types used for the return value of a method or function.
[Return Value](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/return-value.ini.html): Return value is the value returned by a method or function call.
[Return](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/return.ini.html): ``return`` ends the current method, and sends back a produced value to the calling module.
[Return Type Will Change](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/returntypewillchange.ini.html): This is a native PHP attribute.
[Reverse Regex](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/reverse-regex.ini.html): A reverse regex is a library that produces a string based on a regex.
[Revision](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/revision.ini.html): A revision typically refers to a specific state or snapshot of a VCS repository at a particular point in its history.
[rewind()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rewind.ini.html): ``rewind()`` is a native PHP function that sets the file position indicator for a file pointer to the beginning of the file.
[RFC](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rfc.ini.html): The RFC acronym has several usage:
[Request For Change (RFC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rfchange.ini.html): A Request For Change is a document containing a call for an adjustment of a source code.
[Reserved For Future Use (RFU)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rfu.ini.html): RFU stands for Reserved for Future Use.
[Rich Domain Model](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rich-domain-model.ini.html): A rich domain model is one where domain objects, entities, aggregates, value objects, encapsulate both state and the business behaviour that depends on that state.
[Rich Text](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rich-text.ini.html): Rich Text refers to data that is human-readable, yet includes extra information to apply style to that text, such as paragraph, section, font, bold, italic, etc.
[Rigidity](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rigidity.ini.html): Rigidity is when a change in one place forces changes in many other places.
[robots.txt](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/robots.txt.ini.html): The ``robots.
[Rollback](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rollback.ini.html): A rollback is the process of reverting a system, database, or deployment to a previous known-good state after a failure or undesirable change.
[Rounding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rounding.ini.html): Rounding refers to the process of adjusting numerical values to a certain degree of precision.
[Route](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/route.ini.html): A route links an HTTP URL to a request handler.
[Row Polymorphism](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/row-polymorphism.ini.html): Row polymorphism is a type system feature that allows functions to be polymorphic over records or objects with an open set of fields.
[Remote Procedure Call (RPC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rpc.ini.html): RPC is an acronym for Remote Procedure Call.
[RSA](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rsa.ini.html): RSA is the Rivest–Shamir–Adleman algorithm, a widely used method of encryption in the field of Cryptography.
[Rubber Ducking Debugging](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/rubber-ducking.ini.html): Rubber Ducking is a debugging technique, where one explains the bug in human language, to a partner.
[Runtime Checks](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/runtime-checks.ini.html): Runtime checks refer to the process of verifying certain conditions or constraints during the execution of a script.
[Software-as-a-Service (SAAS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/saas.ini.html): SaaS stands for Software as a Service.
[Sandbox](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sandbox.ini.html): An isolated environment where code can be run or tested, without affecting the real system.
[Sanitation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sanitation.ini.html): Sanitation is the process to clean data and remove any unwanted parts.
[Server Application Programming Interface (SAPI)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sapi.ini.html): SAPI stands for Server Application Programming Interface.
[Static Application Security Testing (SAST)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sast.ini.html): Static Application Security Testing, or SAST, is a branch of static analysis, dedicated to security.
[Software Bill Of Material (SBOM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sbom.ini.html): A Software Bill of Materials, aka SBOM, is a formal, machine-readable inventory of all components, libraries, and dependencies used in a software project.
[Static Code Analysis (SCA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sca.ini.html): Static code analysis, or SCA, analyze the source code:
[Scaffolding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scaffolding.ini.html): Scaffolding is the automated generation of PHP code to set up a complex operation.
[Scalar Types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scalar-type.ini.html): Scalar types refer to the basic data types that hold a single value.
[Scaling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scaling.ini.html): Scaling refers to the ability of an application to handle increased workload or traffic while maintaining performance, availability, and reliability.
[Schema](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/schema.ini.html): A schema is a blueprint or structural representation of the organization and structure of a complex dataset.
[Scheme](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scheme.ini.html): Scheme is a format for a string.
[Scope Resolution Operator ::](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scope-resolution-operator.ini.html): The Scope Resolution Operator ``:
[Scope](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/scope.ini.html): The scope, also called context, is the space in which a certain elements are defined.
[Software Development Kit (SDK)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sdk.ini.html): SDK stands for Software Development Kit.
[Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sdlc.ini.html): SDLC stands for Software Development Life Cycle.
[Sealed Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sealed-class.ini.html): A sealed class, or sealed interface, is a class whose set of direct subclasses is restricted and known at compile time.
[Search Engine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/search-engine.ini.html): A search engine is a system that indexes and retrieves data based on queries.
[Second Order SQL Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/second-order-sql-injection.ini.html): A second-order SQL injection, also known as stored SQL injection or persistent SQL injection, is a type of SQL injection attack where the payload is not directly injected into the application's SQL query, but rather into the application's database, from where it is later retrieved and executed within another context, leading to a security vulnerability.
[Secret](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/secret.ini.html): In Computer Science, especially in cybersecurity, cryptography, and software development, a secret refers to any piece of sensitive information that must be kept confidential and protected from unauthorized access.
[Security](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/security.ini.html): Security is a whole matter:
[SeekableIterator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/seekableiterator.ini.html): ``SeekableIterator`` extends the ``Iterator`` interface by adding a ``seek(int $offset)`` method to jump directly to a specific position without iterating through preceding elements.
[Segmentation Fault](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/segmentation-fault.ini.html): ``Segmentation fault`` is the laconic error message that is displayed when PHP crashes.
[Self-documenting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/self-documenting.ini.html): A self-documenting piece of code may be understood without the need to read the documentation.
[Self-reference](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/self-reference.ini.html): A self-reference is a reference into one-self.
[Self](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/self.ini.html): ``self`` is one of the three special keywords that are used to access properties or methods from inside the class definition.
[Semaphore](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sem.ini.html): A semaphore is a synchronization primitive used to control access to shared resources in concurrent systems.
[Semantic Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/semantic-analysis.ini.html): Semantic analysis is the phase of compilation or static analysis that checks the meaning of a program after its structure has been parsed.
[Semantic Versioning](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/semantic-versioning.ini.html): Semantic Versioning is a version numbering scheme that encodes meaning about the changes in each release.
[Semantics](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/semantics.ini.html): Semantics is related with the meaning of things.
[Semicolon ;](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/semicolon.ini.html): Semicolon ``;`` is used to mark the end of a statement.
[Sensitive Parameter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sensitiveparameter.ini.html): This is a native PHP attribute, which tells the engine that a parameter is a sensitive parameter, and should not be displayed by PHP when an error displays the stack trace.
[Sentinel](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sentinel.ini.html): A sentinel is a special, reserved value used to signal a specific condition, such as the end of a sequence, an absence of data, or an error, instead of adding a separate flag or an exception for it.
[Search Engine Optimization (SEO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/seo.ini.html): Search Engine Optimization, or SEO, is the practice of improving a website so that search engines rank it higher in search results.
[Separation Of Concerns](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/separation-of-concerns.ini.html): Separation of concerns is a design principle that advocates dividing a software system into distinct sections, each addressing a distinct concern.
[Separator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/separator.ini.html): Separators are specific characters that ends a previous area, and open a new one.
[Sequence](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sequence.ini.html): A sequence is an ordered collection of values accessed by integer index starting from 0.
[Serde](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/serde.ini.html): ``Serde`` is shorthand for SERialization + DEserialization.
[Serializable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/serializable.ini.html): ``Serializable`` is a PHP native interface, that makes implementing classes use custom code to serialize and unserialized their objects.
[Serialization](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/serialization.ini.html): Serialization refers to the process of converting a data structure or object into a format that can be easily stored, transmitted, or reconstructed later.
[serialize_precision](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/serialize_precision.ini.html): The ``serialize_precision`` PHP ini directive controls the number of significant digits used when serializing float values via ``serialize()`` and ``json_encode()``.
[Server Architecture](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/server-architecture.ini.html): Server architecture refers to the design and organization of the hardware, software, networks, and protocols that make up a server system.
[Server](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/server.ini.html): A server is a piece of computer hardware or software that provides functionality for other programs or devices, called clients.
[Serverless](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/serverless.ini.html): Serverless computing is an execution model where a cloud provider allocates compute resources on demand, scaling to zero when idle.
[Service Container](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/service-container.ini.html): A service container is a design pattern used in software development to manage and provide services as dependencies.
[Service Discovery](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/service-discovery.ini.html): Service discovery is the mechanism by which services in a distributed system find each other's network locations, such as host, port, protocol, dynamically, without hardcoding addresses.
[Service Registry](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/service-registry.ini.html): A service registry is a database of available service instances in a distributed system.
[Service](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/service.ini.html): A service is a class that encapsulates specific functionality, making it available to other parts of an application.
[Session Fixation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/session-fixation.ini.html): A session fixation attack is a type of security exploit where an attacker sets a victim's session identifier to a known value, allowing the attacker to hijack the session once the victim logs in.
[Session Hijacking](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/session-highjacking.ini.html): Session hijacking, also known as session stealing, is an attack where an unauthorized person takes over a user's session.
[Session](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/session.ini.html): Session support consists of a way to preserve certain data across subsequent accesses to the web server.
[SessionHandlerInterface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sessionhandlerinterface.ini.html): ``SessionHandlerInterface`` defines the contract for custom PHP session save handlers.
[SessionIdInterface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sessionidinterface.ini.html): ``SessionIdInterface`` is an optional interface a session handler can implement to generate its own session IDs.
[SessionUpdateTimestampHandlerInterface](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sessionupdatetimestamphandlerinterface.ini.html): ``SessionUpdateTimestampHandlerInterface`` allows session handlers to validate session IDs and control whether the session timestamp is updated on read.
[Set](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/set.ini.html): A set is a data structure that stores unique values with no particular order.
[set_error_handler()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/set_error_handler.ini.html): ``set_error_handler()`` is a native PHP function, which replaces the default error handler from PHP with a custom one.
[setlocale](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/setlocale.ini.html): ``setlocale()`` is a built-in PHP function that sets locale information, affecting string functions such as ``strtolower()``, ``strtoupper()``, ``strcmp()``, ``strftime()`` and number formatting.
[Class Setter Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/setter.ini.html): Setters are methods used to give the values of a property.
[Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sha.ini.html): Secure Hash Algorithm, or SHA, is a family of cryptographic hash functions used to turn data into a fixed-length string called a hash or digest.
[Shallow Clone](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shallow-clone.ini.html): A shallow clone is an object cloning, where the cloned object keeps the same object properties as the original object.
[Shard](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shard.ini.html): Database sharding is a database architecture strategy where a large database is partitioned into smaller, more manageable units called shards.
[Shared Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shared-database.ini.html): The shared database pattern, and more commonly, the shared database anti-pattern, describes multiple services or applications reading from and writing to the same database schema.
[Shared Memory](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shared-memory.ini.html): Shared memory is a technique that allows multiple processes to access the same region of memory, enabling fast inter-process communication, IPC, without the overhead of serialization or network transfers.
[Shared](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shared.ini.html): Shared describes a resource, whether it is memory, state, a database, a library, a connection, or an object, that is accessible by more than one process, thread, request, or component at the same time.
[Shell Exec](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shell-exec.ini.html): Shell execution runs operating system commands from PHP code.
[Shell Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shell-injection.ini.html): Shell injection, also called OS command injection, is a security vulnerability in which attacker-controlled data is passed unsanitised to a shell command, allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the host operating system.
[Shell](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shell.ini.html): A shell is a program that provides an interface for users to interact with the operating system and execute commands.
[shell_exec()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shell_exec.ini.html): ``shell_exec()`` executes a command with the underlying operating system.
[Ship](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ship.ini.html): Shipping, or to ship, refers to the publication of one or more features or services, to the public.
[Shared Memory Operations (SHMOP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shmop.ini.html): ``shmop`` is a PHP extension that gives direct, low-level access to Unix shared memory segments.
[Short Assignations](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/short-assignation.ini.html): In addition to the basic assignment operator, there are combined operators for all of the binary arithmetic, array union and string operators that allow to use a value in an expression and then set its value to the result of that expression.
[Short Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/short-syntax.ini.html): A short syntax is the fusion of an operator with the ``=`` operator.
[Short Tags](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/short-tag.ini.html): PHP uses special tags to identify code that has to be executed.
[Short Ternary Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/short-ternary.ini.html): The expression ``(expr1) ?:
[short_open_tag](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/short_open_tag.ini.html): ``short_open_tag`` is a php.
[Shutdown Function](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shutdown-function.ini.html): The shutdown function is a PHP custom function which is called when the application is shutting down.
[Shutdown](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/shutdown.ini.html): Shutdown is the final phase of a PHP request lifecycle, when the engine tears down everything that was set up to run the script.
[Side Effect](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/side-effect.ini.html): A side effect refers to any change in the state of a program or system that occurs when a function or expression is evaluated, beyond simply returning a value.
[Sign](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sign.ini.html): A signed expression is an expression that starts with the ``+`` or the ``-`` token.
[Signature](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/signature.ini.html): Signature has several meanings:
[Signed Integer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/signed-integer.ini.html): PHP integers are always signed:
[Silent Behavior](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/silent.ini.html): Silent behaviors are expressions that do not raise any error, yet perform a transformation on data, with or without the programmer's knowledge.
[Simple Switch](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/simple-switch.ini.html): A simple switch is a switch where all the cases are simple literals.
[SimpleXML](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/simplexml.ini.html): The SimpleXML extension provides a very simple and easily usable toolset to convert XML to an object that can be processed with normal property selectors and array iterators.
[Single Quotes Strings](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/single-quote.ini.html): Single quoted strings are strings that include a literal value.
[Single](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/single.ini.html): Single usually means that something appears only once.
[Singleton](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/singleton.ini.html): A singleton is a class which can only have one object.
[sitemap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sitemap.ini.html): The ``sitemap`` file is an XML file that lists all the important pages of a website, helping search engines like Google discover, crawl, and index the content more efficiently.
[Service Level Agreement (SLA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sla.ini.html): A Service Level Agreement, or SLA, is a formal contract between a service provider and a client that defines the expected level of service.
[Slash /](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/slash.ini.html): ``/`` is character, used in various situations:
[sleep](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sleep.ini.html): ``sleep()`` is a PHP function which stops the execution for a given number of seconds.
[Slim](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/slim.ini.html): Slim is a lightweight PHP micro-framework designed for building APIs and small web applications.
[Slug](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/slug.ini.html): A slug is a URL-friendly string derived from a human-readable label.
[Smart Cast](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/smart-cast.ini.html): A smart cast, also called compiler-enforced type narrowing, is a feature where the compiler automatically refines the static type of a variable inside a branch, based on a preceding type-check, without requiring the programmer to insert an explicit cast expression.
[Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/smtp.ini.html): Simple Mail Transfer Protocol, aka SMTP, is the standard protocol used for sending emails across the internet.
[Snake Case](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/snake-case.ini.html): Snake case, also written ``snake_case`` is a way of writing names without spaces, where:
[Snapshot](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/snapshot.ini.html): A snapshot captures the complete state of an object or aggregate at a specific point in time.
[Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/snmp.ini.html): SNMP stands for 'Simple Network Management Protocol'.
[SNMP3](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/snmp3.ini.html): ``SNMP3`` The SNMP extension provides a very simple and easily usable toolset for managing remote devices via the Simple Network Management Protocol.
[Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/soa.ini.html): Service-Oriented Architecture, or SOA, is an architectural style where software is composed of discrete, reusable services that communicate over a network through well-defined interfaces.
[Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/soap.ini.html): ``SOAP`` is the acronym for Simple Object Access Protocol.
[Socket](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/socket.ini.html): A socket refers to a communication endpoint that allows two processes to exchange data over a network.
[Software Transactional Memory (STM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/software-transactional-memory.ini.html): Software Transactional Memory, or STM, introduced by Shavit and Touitou in 1995, is a concurrency control mechanism that borrows the transactional semantics from databases and applies them to in-memory operations.
[SOLID](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/solid.ini.html): SOLID is an acronym for a set of OOP design principle.
[Sort](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sort.ini.html): Sorting is the action to put a list of object into a specific order.
[State of the Art (SOTA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sota.ini.html): State of the Art, abbreviated SOTA, refers to the highest level of development achieved in a field at a given point in time.
[Source Code](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/source.ini.html): Source code is the human-readable set of instructions written in a programming language.
[Single Page Application (SPA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/spa.ini.html): SPA's a web application or website that interacts with the user by dynamically rewriting the current page, rather than loading entire new pages from the server.
[Space](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/space.ini.html): Space is the `` `` character:
[Spaceship Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/spaceship.ini.html): Spaceship operator is an operator which reports if a value is larger, equal or smaller than another value.
[SPARQL](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sparql.ini.html): SPARQL is a query language for RDF, Resource Description Framework, data stores.

[Special Types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/special-typehint.ini.html): PHP defines some special types.
[Specification](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/specification.ini.html): A specification is a formal description of expected behaviour, constraints, or requirements.
[Spiral](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/spiral.ini.html): Spiral has two main meanings in the PHP and software engineering world.
[Standard PHP Library (SPL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/spl.ini.html): The Standard PHP Library, the SPL, is a collection of interfaces and classes that are meant to solve common problems.
[SplDoublyLinkedList](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/spldoublylinkedlist.ini.html): The ``SplDoublyLinkedList`` class is an implementation of the concept of doubly linked list.
[SplFileInfo](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splfileinfo.ini.html): ``SplFileInfo`` provides an object-oriented interface to query information about a file or directory:
[SplFileObject](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splfileobject.ini.html): ``SplFileObject`` is an SPL class that offers an object-oriented interface for reading and writing files.
[SplObjectStorage](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splobjectstorage.ini.html): ``SplObjectStorage`` provides a map from objects to arbitrary data, or a set of objects.
[SplObserver](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splobserver.ini.html): ``SplObserver`` is an SPL interface that represents the observer role in the Observer design pattern.
[SplStack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splstack.ini.html): The ``SplStack`` class is part of the Standard PHP Library, SPL.

[SplSubject](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/splsubject.ini.html): ``SplSubject`` is an SPL interface that represents the subject, also known as the observable, role in the Observer design pattern.
[sprintf](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sprintf.ini.html): ``sprintf()`` is a PHP native function, which format a string based on a format string, and one or more values.
[Sqids](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sqids.ini.html): Sqids (formerly known as Hashids) is an open-source library that encodes one or more non-negative integers into short, YouTube-like alphanumeric strings, and decodes them back.
[SQL Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sql-database.ini.html): A SQL database is a database server that process data based on the SQL language.
[SQL Injection](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sql-injection.ini.html): A SQL injection is a vulnerability, where external data are used to change the behavior of a SQL query.
[Structured Query Language (SQL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sql.ini.html): Structured Query Language, SQL, is a database query language, used to access and update data in a relational database management system.
[SQLite3](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sqlite3.ini.html): SQLite is a C-language library that implements a small, fast, self-contained, high-reliability, full-featured, SQL database engine.
[sqrt()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sqrt.ini.html): ``sqrt()`` is a PHP native function, that computes the square root of a number.

[Single Responsability Principle (SRP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/srp.ini.html): Single Responsibility Principle is a coding principle where one component is responsible for one task, and nothing more.
[Static Single Assignment Form (SSA)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ssa.ini.html): SSA stands for Static Single Assignment form.
[sscanf()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sscanf.ini.html): ``sscanf()`` is a built-in PHP function that parses input from a string according to a format string.
[Server Sent Event (SSE)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sse.ini.html): Server-Sent Events, or SSE, is a web technology that allows a server to push real-time updates to a client, often the web browser, over a single HTTP connection.
[Secure Shell (SSH)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ssh.ini.html): SSH, acronym of Secure Shell, is a cryptographic network protocol for operating network services securely over an unsecured network.
[SSH2](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ssh2.ini.html): ``ssh2`` is a PIE extension, built on libssh2, that provides functions to open SSH connections from PHP and use them to execute remote commands, transfer files over SFTP or SCP, forward ports, and manage host authentication.
[Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ssl.ini.html): SSL, the Secure Sockets Layer, is a cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security over a computer network and on the Web.
[Single Sign On (SSO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sso.ini.html): Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication process that allows users to access multiple applications or services with one set of login credentials (username and password).
[Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ssrf.ini.html): Server-Side Request Forgery, or SSRF, is a vulnerability where an attacker induces the server itself to issue an HTTP or other network request to a destination the attacker controls or chooses, by supplying or influencing a URL that the server later fetches.
[Stack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stack.ini.html): A stack may have several meanings:
[Standalone Types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/standalone-type.ini.html): Types are standalone, as they can be use alone, in a type declaration.
[Standard](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/standard.ini.html): A standard, in the context of programming languages and software development, refers to a de facto set of rules, specifications, or guidelines that ensure consistency, interoperability, and quality.
[Star *](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/star.ini.html): ``*`` is a character, used in various situations:
[State](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/state.ini.html): In object-oriented PHP, the state of an object refers to the values of its properties at a given time.
[Stateful](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stateful.ini.html): Stateful refers to the retaining and managing the state or information about a user or a process across multiple requests.
[Stateless](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stateless.ini.html): Stateless refers to a system or protocol that does not retain information about the previous interactions or states of users or devices.
[Statement](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/statement.ini.html): A statement can be an assignment, a function call, a loop, a conditional statement or even a statement that does nothing, an empty statement.
[Static Call](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-call.ini.html): A static call is a call to a structure, where the name of the structure is hardcoded.
[Static Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-class.ini.html): A static class is a class that only holds static methods, properties or constants.
[Static Expression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-expression.ini.html): A static expression is an expression that is wholly defined at coding time.
[Static Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-method.ini.html): Static methods are methods defined with the static keyword.
[Static Property](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-property.ini.html): Static properties are properties defined with the ``static`` keyword.
[Static Variables](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static-variable.ini.html): A static variable is a variable that retains its value across multiple function calls.
[static](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/static.ini.html): The ``static`` keyword has several distinct usages.
[stdclass](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stdclass.ini.html): Stdclass is a native PHP class.
[STDOUT](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stdout.ini.html): ``stdout`` refers to the standard output stream.
[Stealth Generator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stealth-generator.ini.html): A function becomes a generator as soon as the ``yield`` keyword is added to the block of code.
[Steganography](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/steganography.ini.html): Steganography is the practice of concealing a file, message, image, or video within another file, message, image, or video.
[Storage Systems](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/storage-system.ini.html): A storage system is a generic term to describe an external service that store data for a longer time than the current PHP request.
[str_contains()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/str_contains.ini.html): ``str_contains()`` is a PHP native function that checks if a string contains another string.
[str_ends_with()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/str_ends_with.ini.html): ``str_ends_with()`` is a built-in PHP function that checks that the string ends with the specified suffix.
[str_starts_with()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/str_starts_with.ini.html): ``str_starts_with()`` is a built-in PHP function that checks that the string starts with the specified prefix.
[Stream](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stream.ini.html): Streams are a generalisation of the notion of files.
[Streamlining](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/streamlining.ini.html): Streamlining refers to the practice of simplifying code to reduce complexity, remove redundancy, and improve readability and maintainability.
[Strict Comparison](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strict-comparison.ini.html): Strict comparison is a comparison operator or a control structure that checks not only the value but also the type of the value.
[Strict](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strict.ini.html): The word ``strict`` appears in several distinct PHP concepts:
[strict_types](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strict_types.ini.html): ``strict_types`` is an execution directive, that makes PHP more strict when applying the types.
[String Increment](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/string-increment.ini.html): String increment is the PHP feature that applies the ``++`` pre- and post-increment operators on non-numeric strings.
[String Interpolation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/string-interpolation.ini.html): String interpolation is a feature where variables inside a string are dynamically replaced by their value at execution time.
[String](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/string.ini.html): A string is series of characters, where a character is the same as a byte.
[Stringable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stringable.ini.html): ``stringable`` is a type, which represents data that may be used as a string:
[Strpos() Syndrome](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strpos-syndrom.ini.html): The ``strpos()`` syndrome is a classic bug:
[strpos](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strpos.ini.html): ``strpos()`` is a built-in PHP function that finds the position of the first occurrence of a string inside another string.
[strtolower()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strtolower.ini.html): ``strtolower()`` converts all ASCII alphabetic characters in a string to lowercase.
[strtoupper()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/strtoupper.ini.html): ``strtoupper()`` converts all ASCII alphabetic characters in a string to uppercase.
[Struct Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/struct-type.ini.html): A struct is a value type that groups named fields together, as in C, Go, Rust, or Swift.
[Structural Pattern Matching](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/structural-pattern-matching.ini.html): Structural pattern matching, found in Rust, Haskell, Scala, and OCaml, matches a value against a shape that mirrors the value's own structure, and simultaneously binds the pieces it finds to new variables.
[Structural Typing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/structural-typing.ini.html): Structural typing is a type-compatibility rule under which a value satisfies a type purely because it exposes the required shape:
[Structure](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/structure.ini.html): A structure is a complex data or processing routine, such as a class, trait, interface, function, enumeration.
[Stubs Files](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/stubs.ini.html): Stubs files are PHP code, that contains only the definitions of classic PHP structures:
[Sub (prefix)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sub.ini.html): ``sub`` is a prefix, that is added to existing structure, to express that they are depending on a larger similar structure, which acts as a parent 

It applies to several concepts:
[Subclass](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/subclass.ini.html): A ``subclass`` is a class that extends another class.
[Subnamespaces](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/subnamespace.ini.html): In PHP, there is no such notion as subnamespaces.
[subtype](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/subtype.ini.html): A subtype is any of the child types.
[Suffix](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/suffix.ini.html): A suffix is a word extension, placed after the name.
[Sum Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sum-type.ini.html): A sum type is a type whose value is exactly one of a fixed set of cases, where each case may carry a different payload.
[Sunsetting](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/sunsetting.ini.html): Sunsetting a feature is a period of deprecation, where the feature is still available, but is not recommended for usage.
[Superglobal Variables](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/superglobal.ini.html): PHP superglobal variables are predefined variables that are accessible from any scope within a PHP script without the need for any special syntax or function calls.
[supertype](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/supertype.ini.html): A supertype is any of the parent types.
[Supply Chain Attack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/supply-chain-attack.ini.html): Supply Chain Attack is a security attack that uses dependencies to infect a program.
[Supply Chain](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/supply-chain.ini.html): The supply chain is the set of components, libraries, tools and processes that are used to build an application.
[Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/svg.ini.html): SVG, for Scalable Vector Graphics, is an XML-based format for two-dimensional vector graphics.
[Support Vector Machine (SVM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/svm.ini.html): SVM stands for Support Vector Machine.
