[SVN](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/svn.ini.html): SVN, or Subversion, is a version control system, VCS, used for managing and tracking changes to files and directories in software development and other collaborative environments.
[Swagger](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/swagger.ini.html): Swagger is a toolset for designing, documenting, and consuming REST APIs.
[Swap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/swap.ini.html): Swap may be two things:
[Switch Case](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/switch-case.ini.html): A switch case, also called case, is an option in a ``switch()`` or a ``match()`` expression.
[Switch Default](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/switch-default.ini.html): A switch default, also called default, is the catch-all option in a ``switch()`` or a ``match()`` expression.
[Switch](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/switch.ini.html): The ``switch`` expression branches evaluation based on an equality check of a value.
[Swoole](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/swoole.ini.html): ``Swoole`` is a PIE extension that turns PHP into an event-driven, asynchronous, coroutine-capable runtime, replacing the traditional process-per-request model with long-running server processes that handle HTTP, WebSocket, TCP, and UDP connections directly, without a separate web server such as Nginx or PHP-FPM in front.
[Symbolic Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/symbolic-analysis.ini.html): Symbolic analysis, also called symbolic execution, is a static analysis technique that executes a program with symbolic values instead of concrete inputs.
[Symfony](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/symfony.ini.html): Symfony is a popular PHP framework:
[Synchronous](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/synchronous.ini.html): Synchronous PHP refers to the traditional way PHP applications execute code:
[Syntax Error](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/syntax-error.ini.html): A syntax error happens when the text could not be parsed into tokens by PHP.
[Syntax](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/syntax.ini.html): Syntax refers to the set of rules that defines the structure of valid PHP code.
[System Call](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/system-call.ini.html): A system call is a call to an operating system function.
[System Event](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/system-event.ini.html): System events, also known as signals, are software interrupts sent to a program to indicate that an important event has occurred.
[System](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/system.ini.html): The system, or the operating system, is the environment where PHP is running.
[Tabulation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tabulation.ini.html): A tabulation is a whitespace character, that often represents the equivalent of four spaces characters.
[Taint Analysis](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/taint.ini.html): Taint analysis is a security technique used to identify and trace potentially unsafe or untrusted data as it flows through a program.
[Tarpit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tarpit.ini.html): A tar pit is a deliberate slowdown of abusive or unwanted requests to waste an attacker's time and resources.
[Task Runner](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/task-runner.ini.html): A task runner refers to a tool or framework that automates repetitive tasks, such as building, testing, and deploying PHP applications.
[Task](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/task.ini.html): A task is simply a unit of work:
[Total Cost Of Ownership (TCO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tco.ini.html): Total Cost of Ownership, aka TCO, is a financial estimate that helps businesses and individuals calculate the direct and indirect costs associated with owning and operating software, hardware, equipment, or even a service, over its entire lifecycle.
[TCP](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tcp.ini.html): ``TCP``, which stands for Transmission Control Protocol, is one of the main protocols in the Internet Protocol ``IP`` suite.
[Tech Island](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tech-island.ini.html): A tech island is an organisational behaviour where a team replicates tools, libraries, or infrastructure internally rather than depending on external ones.
[Tech Silo](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tech-silo.ini.html): A tech silo is a situation where a team or department develops and operates its technology stack in isolation, without sharing tools, knowledge, or practices with the rest of the organisation.
[Technical Reference](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/technical-reference.ini.html): A technical reference is a type of documentation that describes the precise behaviour of a system's components:
[Telemetry](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/telemetry.ini.html): Telemetry refers to the automated collection and transmission of data about performance, behavior, and health of an application.
[Tempest](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tempest.ini.html): Tempest is the framework that gets out of your way.
[Template Metaprogramming (TMP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/template-metaprogramming.ini.html): Template metaprogramming, or TMP, is a technique in which a language's template or generics system is used to perform computations at compile time, producing specialised code as output.
[Template](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/template.ini.html): A template is a preset document, filled with values in placeholders, to display the resulting data.
[Temporal Replay](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/temporal-replay.ini.html): Temporal replay is the technique of re-executing a recorded sequence of timestamped events, in their original order, to reconstruct the state a system had at any point in time, or to reproduce a past execution for debugging, testing, or auditing.
[Temporary Expression](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/temporary-expression.ini.html): A temporary expression is an expression which is used on the spot, and it is not stored in a variable, or a container for later reuse.
[Temporary](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/temporary.ini.html): Temporary describes resources, values, or storage that exist only for a bounded duration and are automatically discarded when that boundary is crossed.
[Ternary Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ternary.ini.html): The expression ``(expr1) ? (expr2) :
[Test Data Provider](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/test-data-provider.ini.html): A test data provider is a mechanism or component that supplies the necessary data for executing test cases.
[Test-Driven Development](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/test-driven-development.ini.html): Test-driven development is a software development approach where tests are written before the code.
[Test Framework](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/test-framework.ini.html): A test framework is a library or tool that provides the infrastructure for writing, organising, and running automated tests.
[Test Pyramid](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/test-pyramid.ini.html): The Test Pyramid is a concept in software testing that represents the ideal distribution of different types of tests in a software testing strategy.
[Test](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/test.ini.html): Tests are tools that monitor the behavior of a code.
[Testable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/testable.ini.html): A piece of code is testable when it may have related tests, that checks its behavior.
[testdox](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/testdox.ini.html): TestDox, which stands for TestDocs, is an output format for unit test frameworks, where the name of the tests, aka the methods, are converted into a human readable string.
[Text](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/text.ini.html): Text refers to data that is human-readable and composed of characters, such as letters, numbers, punctuation, according to a specific encoding, such as ASCII or UTF-8.
[Thin Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/thin-method.ini.html): A thin method is an informal term used to describe a method that contains very little logic of its own and mostly delegates work to another method or object.
[Thin](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/thin.ini.html): Thin is an informal qualifier applied to a class that has very few responsibilities:
[Thread](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/thread.ini.html): A thread is a set of instructions, designed to be executed independently from the parent process.
[Three Dots](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/three-dots.ini.html): Three dots, is an operator with several usages and names.
[Throughput](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/throughput.ini.html): Throughput is the number of operations a system can process per unit of time.
[throw](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/throw.ini.html): Throw is the keyword which raise an exception.
[Throwable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/throwable.ini.html): ``Throwable`` is a PHP native interface, which is required for any object to be used with thrown, such as ``Exception`` and ``Error``.
[Tick](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tick.ini.html): Ticks are events that occur for a group of statements, executed by PHP.
[Tideways](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tideways.ini.html): Tideways is a PHP profiler and application performance monitoring tool.
[Tilde ~](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tilde.ini.html): The tilde ``~`` is an operator and a wildcard.
[Time Travel](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/time-travel.ini.html): Time travel debugging is a technique in software development that allows the program to step backward as well as forward through its execution path.
[Timing Attack](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/timing-attack.ini.html): A timing attack is a type of side-channel attack where an attacker exploits variations in the time it takes for a system to perform operations to infer secret information.
[Transport Layer Security (TLS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tls.ini.html): ``TLS``, the Transport Layer Security is a cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security over a computer network and on the Web.
[Time Of Check To Time Of Use (TOCTOU)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/toctou.ini.html): TOCTOU, which stands for Time Of Check To Time Of Use, is a class of bug where there is a time interval between checking a resource and then using it.
[TODO](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/todo.ini.html): ``TODO`` is a conventional comment marker used in source code to flag unfinished work, known limitations, or planned improvements.
[Token](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/token.ini.html): A PHP token is the smallest unit of code that has meaning in the language.
[Tokenizer](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tokenizer.ini.html): The PHP Tokenizer is a built-in extension that breaks down PHP source code into its fundamental components called tokens.
[Tom's Obvious Minimal Language (TOML)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/toml.ini.html): TOML stands for ``Tom's Obvious, Minimal Language``.
[Tool](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tool.ini.html): A tool in the PHP ecosystem refers to any standalone application, script, or library that assists developers during the software development lifecycle.
[Token Oriented Object Notation (TOON)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/toon.ini.html): ``TOON``, which stands for ``Token Oriented Object Notation``, is a compact, human-readable encoding of the JSON data model that minimizes tokens and makes structure easy for models to follow.
[Trace](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/trace.ini.html): A trace is the list of called functions during an execution.
[Trailing Comma](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/trailing-comma.ini.html): The trailing comma is the possibility to leave the last element of a list empty.
[Trait](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/trait.ini.html): Traits are a mechanism for code reuse in single inheritance languages.
[Transaction](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/transaction.ini.html): A transaction is a sequence of database operations that is treated as a single atomic unit.
[Transpile](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/transpile.ini.html): Transpiling, short for "source-to-source compiling," is a process in software development where source code written in one programming language is transformed into equivalent code in another programming language.
[Traversable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/traversable.ini.html): Traversable is a specific PHP interface that represents an object that can be iterated over.
[Traversal](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/traversal.ini.html): A traversal is the process of listing all elements in a data structure.
[Tree](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tree.ini.html): A tree is a datastructure which represents data as a set of connected nodes, where each node has only one parent.
[Treemap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/treemap.ini.html): Treemaps are a visualization tool used to understand the relative weight of partitions.
[trigger_error()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/trigger_error.ini.html): ``trigger_error()`` is a PHP native function, which raises an error, in the style of PHP engine.
[trim](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/trim.ini.html): The ``trim()`` function is used to remove whitespaces from the beginning and end of a string, at the same time.
[Triple](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/triple.ini.html): Triple usually means that something appears only three times in a row.
[Troubleshoot](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/troubleshoot.ini.html): To troubleshoot is to diagnostic and correct a problem in a piece of software.
[True](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/true.ini.html): ``true`` has two usages, as PHP keyword:
[Truthy](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/truthy.ini.html): A truthy value is a value that evaluate to true, when cast to a boolean.
[Try-catch](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/try-catch.ini.html): Try-catch is a PHP command, which run a block of code, and catch some configured exceptions that the block may throw.
[Time To Live (TTL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ttl.ini.html): Time To Live, abbreviated TTL, is a value that defines how long a piece of data remains valid before it expires and must be refreshed or discarded.
[Terminal User Interface (TUI)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tui.ini.html): A Terminal User Interface, or TUI for short, is a type of user interface that uses text-based graphics and keyboard interaction within a terminal or console, providing a richer experience than a plain CLI while avoiding the overhead of a graphical desktop.
[Tuple](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tuple.ini.html): A tuple is a fixed-size, ordered collection of values, where each position may hold a different type, and the type of the whole tuple is the sequence of its element types, such as Rust's ``(i32, String, bool)`` or Python's ``(1, 'a', True)``.
[Tutorial](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/tutorial.ini.html): Tutorial is a type of documentation.
[Type Checking](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-checking.ini.html): Type checking is the process of verifying that the types of expressions, variables, arguments, and return values are consistent with their declarations and usage.
[Type Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-class.ini.html): A type class, as found in Haskell and similar languages, is a named set of functions that a type must implement in order to belong to that class.
[Contravariance](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-contravariance.ini.html): Contravariance allows a parameter type to be less specific in a child method, than that of its parent.
[Covariance](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-covariance.ini.html): Covariance allows a child's method to return a more specific type than the return type of its parent's method.
[Type Coverage](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-coverage.ini.html): Type coverage reports how much of the PHP code has types.
[Property Type Declaration](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-declaration-property.ini.html): Type declaration property, also known as typed properties, is a feature introduced in version 7.
[Types Erasure](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-erasure.ini.html): Type Erasure is the removal of type annotations from the source code when going to production.
[Type Inference](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-inference.ini.html): Type inference is the ability of a tool, such as a static analyzer or the PHP engine, to deduce the type of an expression, variable, or return value without an explicit type declaration.
[Type Juggling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-juggling.ini.html): Type juggling is the emblematic PHP feature, where data has no specific type definition, and its type may change depending on the situation.
[Type Narrowing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-narrowing.ini.html): Type narrowing is the general process of refining the type of a value to a more specific subtype within a scope, based on information gathered from a preceding check:
[Type System](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-system.ini.html): A type system is a formal set of rules that assigns a type to every expression in a program and uses those types to determine which operations are valid, how values are stored, and what errors can be detected before or during execution.
[Type Widening](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type-widening.ini.html): Type widening is the conversion of a value or a declared type toward a broader, more general one:
[Type System](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/type.ini.html): Type declarations can be added to function arguments, return values, and, as of PHP 7.
[Typed Constant](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/typed-constant.ini.html): A typed constant is a class constant that holds an explicit type, like a property or an argument.
[Typed Property](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/typed-property.ini.html): A typed property has a type in its definition.
[TypeError](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/typeerror.ini.html): A type error refers to an error that occurs when there is a mismatch or violation of data types.
[Type Error](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/typerror.ini.html): A ``TypeError`` is an error that occurs when there is a mismatch between the expected data type and the actual data type of a variable or value in your code.
[Typo](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/typo.ini.html): A typo is short for ``typographical error``.
[User Datagram Protocol (UDP)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/udp.ini.html): UDP, or User Datagram Protocol, is a core communication protocol used on the Internet, alongside TCP.
[Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier (ULID)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/ulid.ini.html): ULID is a Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier.
[Unary Operator](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unary.ini.html): A unary operator is an operator that operates on a single operand.
[Unbacked Enum](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unbacked-enum.ini.html): Unbacked enums are enumeration without a string or integer equivalent for each of its cases.
[Undefined](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/undefined.ini.html): Something ``undefined`` has not been defined yet.
[Underflow](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/underflow.ini.html): Underflow happens when a value goes below a limit:
[Underscore](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/underscore.ini.html): Underscore is the ``_`` character.
[UnexpectedValueException](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unexpectedvalueexception.ini.html): The ``UnexpectedValueException`` exception is thrown if a value does not match with a set of expected values.
[UnhandledMatchError](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unhandledmatcherror.ini.html): An ``UnhandledMatchError`` error is thrown when the subject passed to a match expression is not handled by any case of the match expression.
[Unicode](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unicode.ini.html): Unicode is a standard to represent text.
[Uninitialized](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uninitialized.ini.html): A data container, such as variable, property, array index is uninitialized, when it is used for reading, while no value was provided so far.
[Union Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/union-type.ini.html): Union types refer to the ability to specify multiple possible types for a property, parameter, or return value.
[Unique Identifier](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unique-identifier.ini.html): A unique identifier is a value that distinguishes one entity from all others within a given scope.

[Unit Test](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unit-test.ini.html): Unit tests are tests that verify how a coherent piece of code work.
[UnitEnum](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unitenum.ini.html): The ``UnitEnum`` interface identifies any enumeration.
[Unpacking](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unpacking.ini.html): Unpacking has two meanings:
[Unreachable Code](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unreachable-code.ini.html): Unreachable code refers to a section of code within a program that can never be executed during the program's runtime.
[Unserialization](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unserialize.ini.html): Unserialization is the extraction of an object from a string representation of an object.
[unset()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unset.ini.html): ``unset()`` removes a variable, an array item or an property.
[Unused](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/unused.ini.html): Unused is a state of a PHP structure.
[User Operations For Zend (UOPZ)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uopz.ini.html): The ``UOPZ`` extension, User Operations for Zend, exposes Zend Engine functionality normally used at compilation and execution time in order to allow modification of the internal structures that represent PHP code, and for user code to interact with the VM.
[Upgrade](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/upgrade.ini.html): An upgrade refers to the process of updating or replacing an existing installation of PHP or any library or component with a newer version.
[File Upload](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/upload.ini.html): PHP is able to receive files as part of a form submission.
[URI Class](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uri-class.ini.html): The ``URI`` class is the class provided by the uri extension.
[URI Extension](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uri-extension.ini.html): ``uri`` is an extension that provides APIs to securely parse and modify URIs and URLs according to the RFC 3986 and WHATWG URL standards.
[Universal Resource Identifier (URI)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uri.ini.html): Universal Resource Identifier is a unique sequence of characters that identifies a logical or physical resource used by web technologies.
[Universal Resource Locator (URL)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/url.ini.html): Universal Resource Locator is a string that represents a resource, where to find it, how to access it.
[Use Alias](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/use-alias.ini.html): The ``use`` operator can create aliases for a class, function or constant.
[Use In Traits](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/use-trait.ini.html): ``use`` is a keyword for traits and classes:
[Use](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/use.ini.html): The use operator has three distinct usages.
[Used](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/used.ini.html): Used is a state of a PHP structure.
[Useless](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/useless.ini.html): Some piece of code is useless when it doesn't bring any feature to the code.
[Userland](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/userland.ini.html): A ``userland`` feature, or definition, is a piece of code defined with PHP code, by one of the PHP developer.
[UTF-8](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/utf-8.ini.html): UTF-8 is a character encoding standard.
[UUencode Encoding](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uuencode.ini.html): Uuencode, short for ``Unix-to-Unix encoding``, is a binary-to-text encoding mechanism originally designed to transfer binary data over mediums that only support text, such as email or newsgroups.
[Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/uuid.ini.html): UUID is a Universally Unique IDentifier.
[Validation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/validation.ini.html): Validation is the process to check that a data conform to a specific pattern or set of constraints.
[Value Object](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/value-object.ini.html): A value object is a design pattern used to represent immutable objects that encapsulate a set of related values or attributes.
[Value Type](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/value-type.ini.html): A value type, as in C#, Swift, or Go, is copied in full whenever it is assigned or passed to a function, rather than shared by reference; two variables holding a value type never alias the same storage.
[Value](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/value.ini.html): A value is the piece of data that is inside a container, such as a variable, an array, a property or a constant.
[ValueError](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/valueerror.ini.html): A ``ValueError`` is thrown when the type of an argument is correct but the value of it is incorrect.
[Var](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/var.ini.html): ``var`` keyword for introducing PHP properties in classes.
[var_dump()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/var_dump.ini.html): ``var_dump()`` displays the information in a variable or any data container.
[var_export()](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/var_export.ini.html): ``var_export()`` is a PHP native function that produces a representation of the passed data as PHP code.
[Variable Arguments](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable-argument.ini.html): Variable arguments refers to a methodcall where the list of argument is depends on the call, rather than the signature of the method.
[Variable Scope](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable-scope.ini.html): Variable scope is the context in which the variable is available.
[Variable Shadowing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable-shadowing.ini.html): Variable shadowing is the confusion between variables of the same name, but of different context.
[Variable Variables](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable-variable.ini.html): Variable variables refer to a feature that allows to use the value of a variable as the name of another variable.
[Variables](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable.ini.html): Variables are used to store and manipulate data.
[variable_order](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variable_order.ini.html): The ``variable_order`` directive sets the order in which the values are assigned to the ``$_REQUEST`` variable.
[Variadic](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/variadic.ini.html): Three dots, aka ellipsis, is an operator to spread or collect array values.
[VCS Commit](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vcs-commit.ini.html): A commit is a snapshot of source code at a specific point in time.
[Version Control System (VCS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vcs.ini.html): ``VCS`` stands for Version Control System.
[Vector Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vector-database.ini.html): A vector database is a type of database designed to store, manage, and search vector embedding, which are numerical representations of data such as text, images, audio, or video.
[Vendor Lock-in](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vendor-lockin.ini.html): Vendor lock-in is a situation where a user becomes dependent on a specific vendor's products or services, making it difficult, expensive, or risky to switch to another provider.
[Version](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/version.ini.html): In the context of software development, a version refers to a specific iteration or release of a software product or application.
[Vertical Scaling](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vertical-scaling.ini.html): Vertical scaling, also called scaling up, means increasing the resources:
[View In Presentation](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/view-presentation.ini.html): A view is the presentation layer in a MVC model.
[SQL Views](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/view-sql.ini.html): Views in a SQL server is a virtual table, build as the result of a SQL query.
[View](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/view.ini.html): A view may be several things:
[Virtual Machine (VM)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/virtual-machine.ini.html): A virtual machine, or ``VM``, or also runtime, is a software-based emulation of a physical computer.
[Virtual Property](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/virtual-property.ini.html): A virtual property is a class property, whose property hooks don't use the value.
[Visibility](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/visibility.ini.html): Properties, methods and classes may have a visibility.
[Visual Debt](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/visual-debt.ini.html): Visual debt is all the elements that hampers the readability of a PHP code source, even at the cost of source consistence.
[Value Object (VO)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vo.ini.html): ``VO`` stands for Value Object:
[(void) Cast](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/void-cast.ini.html): The ``(void)`` cast is a special cast.
[Void](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/void.ini.html): Void is a return type.
[Vulnerability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/vulnerability.ini.html): A vulnerability is a weakness or flaw in a computer system, software, hardware, or even a process that can be exploited by an attacker to perform unauthorized actions.
[WAL](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wal.ini.html): Write-Ahead Logging, also called WAL, is a standard technique for ensuring data integrity in databases.
[Warning](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/warning.ini.html): A warning is a non-fatal runtime error that alerts developers about a problem in the code, but doesn't stop script execution at that point.
[Waterfall](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/waterfall.ini.html): Waterfall is a sequential software development methodology where each phase must be completed before the next one begins.
[Weak Cryptography](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/weak-cryptography.ini.html): A weak cryptography vulnerability occurs when an application uses outdated, poorly designed, or insufficient cryptographic algorithms, protocols, or implementations to protect sensitive data.
[Weak References](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/weak-reference.ini.html): Weak references allow the programmer to retain a reference to an object which does not prevent the object from being destroyed.
[Weakmap](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/weakmap.ini.html): ``WeakMap`` is a special object type:
[Web Application](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/web-app.ini.html): A web application is software that runs in a web browser and is delivered over HTTP/HTTPS from a web server.
[Web-spider](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/web-spider.ini.html): A web spider, also called a web crawler or bot, is a program that automatically browses the internet and collects information from websites.
[World Wide Web (WWW)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/web.ini.html): The WWW stands for World Wide Web:
[Webhook](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/webhook.ini.html): A webhook is an HTTP callback mechanism where a server notifies a client by sending an HTTP POST request to a pre-registered URL when a specific event occurs.
[WebRTC](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/webrtc.ini.html): WebRTC, short for Web Real-Time Communication, is an open-source technology that enables real-time communication directly between web browsers or applications without the need for plugins or external software.
[Webscraping](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/webscraping.ini.html): Web scraping is the process of extracting data from websites.
[Webserver](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/webserver.ini.html): A web server is a computer system or software application that delivers web pages to users over the internet.
[Webshell](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/webshell.ini.html): A webshell is a malicious script uploaded to a web server that allows an attacker to remotely control the server using a web browser.
[Websocket](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/websocket.ini.html): WebSockets are a communication protocol that enables full-duplex, real-time communication between a client, such as a a web browser, and a server over a single, persistent TCP connection.
[Write Everything Twice (WET)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wet.ini.html): Write Everything Twice, WET, is a principle of software development aimed at reducing abstraction levels and decoupling code.
[WHATWG](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/whatwg.ini.html): WHATWG, the Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group, is a community that develops and maintains web standards, including the HTML Living Standard, DOM, Fetch, URL, and Streams specifications.
[While](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/while.ini.html): While is a loop structure.
[Whitespace](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/whitespace.ini.html): Whitespace refers to spaces, tabulations, and newline characters:
[Wildcard](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wildcard.ini.html): The wildcard, for example the ``*``, ``%``, ``_``, ``~`` characters, is a placeholder that matches anything.
[Class Wither Method](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wither.ini.html): ``with`` is a prefix, that tells the reader that the method returns a copy of the object, and not the object itself.
[Word Combination](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/word-combinaison.ini.html): Word combination is the technique to build a new word by putting one or several words next to each other, and making the whole a distinct or specialized entity from the individual elements.
[Workflow](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/workflow.ini.html): A workflow is a sequence of tasks or steps that are performed to accomplish a specific goal.
[Worktree](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/worktree.ini.html): A worktree is a Git feature that allows multiple working directories to be linked to a single repository.
[Design Pattern Wrapper](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-designpattern.ini.html): A wrapper is also a design pattern, also called an adaptor.
[file://](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-file.ini.html): The ``file:
[ftp://](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-ftp.ini.html): The ``ftp:
[http://](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-http.ini.html): The ``http:
[Wrapper Pattern](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-pattern.ini.html): The wrapper pattern is also called decorator or adapter.
[php://](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-php.ini.html): The ``php:
[Stream Wrapper](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper-stream.ini.html): A stream wrapper is additional code which tells the stream how to handle specific protocols and encodings.
[Wrapper](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/wrapper.ini.html): A wrapper may be two things:
[Writable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/writable.ini.html): Something is writable when a new value may be assigned to it, in the correct context.
[Writeable](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/writeable.ini.html): Something is writeable when its current value may be modified.
[Writing](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/writing.ini.html): Writing is the action to replace a value in a data container with another one.
[Xdebug](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xdebug.ini.html): Xdebug is an extension for PHP, and provides a range of features to improve the PHP development experience.
[XML Database](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xml-database.ini.html): An XML database is a database which uses XML for the storage format.
[Extensible Markup Language (XML)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xml.ini.html): XML, Extensible Markup Language, is a markup language similar to HTML, but without predefined tags to use.
[XMLReader](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xmlreader.ini.html): The ``XMLReader`` extension is an XML Pull parser.
[XMLwriter](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xmlwriter.ini.html): The ``XMLWriter`` PHP extension is a built-in extension that provides a simple and efficient way to generate XML documents.
[Cross Site Scripting (XSS)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xss.ini.html): It's a web security vulnerability where an attacker injects malicious JavaScript into a website, and that script then runs in other users' browsers.
[XXTEA](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/xxtea.ini.html): ``XXTEA`` is a symmetric encryption algorithm that stands for ``Corrected Block TEA``:
[You Are Not Going To Need It (YAGNI)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/yagni.ini.html): YAGNI advises developers to avoid adding functionality or code until it is explicitly required.
[YAML Ain't Markup Language (YAML)](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/yaml.ini.html): ``YAML``, which stands for ``YAML Ain't Markup Language``, is a human-readable data serialization format.
[yield from Keyword](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/yield-from.ini.html): The ``yield from`` keyword is used to delegate the iteration control of one generator to another.
[Yield](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/yield.ini.html): In its simplest form, a yield statement looks much like a return statement, except that instead of stopping execution of the function and returning, yield instead provides a value to the code looping over the generator and pauses execution of the generator function.
[Yoda Condition](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/yoda.ini.html): Yoda condition is a way to write condition by using any literal value on the left, instead of the right.
[zend.assertions](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zend-assertions.ini.html): ``zend.
[Zend Engine](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zend-engine.ini.html): The Zend Engine is the core runtime engine of PHP:
[Zero-code](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zero-code.ini.html): Zero-code, also known as no-code, refers to development platforms and tools that allow building applications without writing any source code.
[Zero Cost](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zero-cost.ini.html): ``zero cost`` most commonly refers to zero-cost assertions, a feature introduced in version 7.
[Zero Day Vulnerability](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zero-day.ini.html): A Zero Day vulnerability is a vulnerability which has no counter measure yet.
[zero](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zero.ini.html): Zero is the ``0`` integer.
[zip Extension](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zip.ini.html): The zip extension provides functions to handle the zip compression format:
[zlib extension](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zlib.ini.html): The ``zlib`` extension provides functions to handle the zlib compression format:
[Zombie Code](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zombie-code.ini.html): Zombie code refers to sections of code that are executed, but whose results are discarded.
[Zval](https://php-dictionary.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dictionary/zval.ini.html): ``ZVAL`` is a C structure, that represents data in the PHP engine.
